Estella's work experiences are rather extensive. I wanted to learn more about her past but she remained exceptionally quiet during the signing process and exhibited zero intents of communication apart from answering the questions I asked. And even amongst these limited things we talked about, I find most of her answers rather difficult to interpret... Fortunately, no issues of principle have been identified. However, given the positive reputation of her superior officer Miss Akekuri and the latter's guarantee, we shall overlook potential risks of personnel management that may result from Estella's lack of communication abilities. We hereby approve Estella's employment. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

4380271354006114390: During the First Aggeloi War, the TGCC was in charge of maintaining the Talos-Terra lifeline. Now that the war has ended, truck convoys stranded in the alien world continued to roar across the roads, making commercial economies, messenger-based courier services, and modern lifestyles possible in Talos-II. With hands tightly gripped around the steering wheel, the truckers cruised across the lands with headlights blazing, carrying containers filled with supplies and resources to link the growing camps, villages, and towns upon the new world. Though the industrial giants of Old Terra remain a dominant force, the truckers who earned the trust and respect of the Talosians during the war became masters of their own destiny. And thus they transformed into the Conductors steering the great wheel of the TGCC.

<@profile.key>Operator Catcher (ID name: Ruy van Catcher) was born to a military family in the Civilization Band. Following Operator Akekuri's applications, Catcher is now a member of Operations Team Z7. 

Existing operational feedback reports indicate that Mr. Catcher is the most reliable operator of Z7. He has never been absent from duty, always hands in his reports on time, does not engage in habits of vice, and is an exceptional team player. 
However, we have noticed that Mr. Catcher has a tendency to place extremely severe demands on himself (which may be a result of his childhood influences). Simply put, he often forces himself to take on operational duties and goals that were not originally assigned to him. When assigning tasks to Mr. Catcher, we advise referencing, where possible, the recommendations of Op Team Z7 Captain Akekuri. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

4612261786488988459: Recently, our correspondent conducted a tour of Valley IV that lies at the fringes of the Civilization Band. Numerous UWST crew mates and frontiersfolk were interviewed to better understand the food, accommodations, and medical amenities available in Valley IV. ...Many companies have resident liaisons and agents operating in the frontier. When a Blight disaster struck this region, most of them joined various forms of search and rescue operations. A company named Endfield Industries demonstrated their effectiveness in handling various disaster-related crises. A reliable source revealed that many field operators of Endfield are veteran combatants and elites of their field, making them incredibly skilled in handling the challenges posed by the extremely hazardous environments. To date (the time when this article is being written), this Endfield "troubleshooting" team has handled and mediated more than a dozen Blight-induced conflicts and emergencies for the residents of Valley IV. ...We can even say that Endfield played an instrumental role in ensuring regional stability as well as the efficiency of post-disaster rebuilding. — And this concludes our Valley IV report from our special correspondent.

4711117997990448919:
Though this wasn't the first time they talked about Bander Scavs, I never really saw one myself.
People can't really appreciate how difficult life could be out there on Talos-II. Band cities and neighborhoods must work closely together to survive, yet many Banders chose to leave the city behind to live as a nomad or prospector in the frontiers.
I myself wasn't too surprised by this. These people had lost too much. I interviewed around a dozen refugees last week from a disaster-stricken neighborhood that ran out of food. They had no other choice.
However, there were also some who refused to live in a world of law or were simply sociopaths. These individuals preferred a life filled with constant violence and danger.
Regardless of their intents, no Bander nomads could truly give up the touch of civilization. Also last week, I saw them craft weapons with a scavenged machine tool. I was extremely curious how they managed to get their hands on a working lathe. The answer was simple. They got lucky with a scavenging run through a Bander city dumpsite. These landfills provided for their every need.
Turned out that Bander Scav was a pretty accurate name for these people.
If you're reading this, tell that little Bander journalist to stop stalking me. People who complain about sleeping bags do not belong in the wildlands. She should get back to her cushy Bander place and stick to being a celebrity tabloid writer.

4833079329798838178:
<@profile.key>Operator Fluorite once encouraged local civilians to violently resist a criminal organization and helped an Endfield field operations team escape certain threats. Operator Akekuri thus endorsed Fluorite's employment with Endfield Industries. She is now a member of Operations Team Z7. No new criminal records have been filed against the Operator since then. 

Pay particular attention to Fluorite in social settings. Her "personal grudges" are usually not as significant as we make them, but conversations with her may take unexpected turns. Fortunately, Fluorite is not averse to being a listener. Since we place greater importance on an operator's combat capabilities, her peculiarity is not particularly difficult to handle. 
However, she seized the initiative and answered every question on the list before I formally delivered it to her. In fact, she even started laughing before I could complete my engaging answers. This situation was not predicted by my algorithms. Hence, this interview has been added to the archives of unique case studies for further analysis. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass -</>
Every item on the Protocol Pass is gathered from official operator interviews conducted by the Arsenal Engineers. These items include support projects that everyone hopes to see.
Activating each seasonal Protocol Pass unlocks the [Basic Supply] tier by default.
The [Protocol Customized] tier can be purchased, while the [Originium Supply] tier can be unlocked by spending [Origeometry].

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass EXP and Levels -</>
There are 2 major ways to earn Protocol Pass EXP, which then levels up the Protocol Pass and grants you rewards.
· Complete Pass Missions (some missions will become available after meeting certain criteria).
· Take part in [Operational Manual - Daily Tasks] and claim the max daily Activity Points rewards.
※ If max Activity Points is not reached for the day, 1 Vacation Tag will be activated at 04:00 (Server Time) of the following day. Up to 3 tags can be activated.
※ After earning max Activity Points and claiming the rewards, 1 activated Vacation Tag will be consumed to give double Pass EXP (Daily task rewards are not affected by this).

Even after maxing the seasonal Protocol Pass level, you can still earn Pass EXP with the aforementioned methods and acquire cyclical rewards. There is no limit to earning Pass EXP that exceed the level cap.

※ Special EXP bonus details: Unlocking [Originium Supply] / [Protocol Customized] tiers of the seasonal Protocol Pass grants +3% / +3% Pass EXP bonus respectively, for a total of +6% Pass EXP bonus when both tiers are unlocked.

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Missions Details -</>
· Weekly Missions: During the Protocol Pass season, weekly missions become available every 7 days. Weekly missions will be reset when the pass season ends.
· Horizons Missions: These are a special type of Protocol Pass missions that are permanently available. Horizons Missions progress will not be reset when the pass season ends, meaning that these missions can be performed during any pass season to claim their rewards. When all Horizons Missions are completed, its special screen will be closed at 04:00 (server time) of the following day.

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Weapons Details -</>
For each pass season, the [Originium Supply] and [Protocol Customized] tiers each contain a special weapon supply.
Every pass season has its own weapon supplies. In other words, weapon supplies from a current season may give a list of weapon choices that differ from those of previous seasons. Also, lists of weapon choices from seasons already released will not change.
[Protocolized Weapon Patterns] can raise the potential of any weapon provided by Protocol Pass weapon supplies, including weapon supplies from future pass seasons.

<@intru.bold>- Special Rules -</>
When the Protocol Pass season ends, any unclaimed pass level rewards shall be sent to your Inbox.
For excessive purchases of [Protocol Customized] due to special circumstances, rewards from [Protocol Customized] tier will not stack. Instead, an amount of [Origeometry] based on the value of the purchase will be returned to you.

<@intru.bold>- Exchange Crate-o-Surprise Details -</>
[Exchange Crates-o-Surprise] are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward and a random progression material reward.

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates
· Exchange Crate-o-Surprise [M]
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%)
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%)

<@intru.bold>- Cycling Crates-o-Surprise Details -</>
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] come in 2 types: (1) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M] and (2) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L].
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] are earned from the cycling rewards, and are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward; a random progression material reward; and a random Sanity Usage Permit reward. 

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M]
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%)
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%)
Sanity Usage Permits: Sanity Usage Permit×1 (90%) / Sanity Usage Permit×2 (10%)
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L]
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×30 (5%) / Oroberyl×25 (35%) / Oroberyl×20 (60%)
Progression Materials: T-Creds×12000 (12.5%) / T-Creds×7500 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×2 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×1 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×5 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×3 (12.5%) / Protohedron×5 (12.5%) / Protohedron×3 (12.5%)

4879358766779101910:
<@profile.key>What do I think of the north? Worst place ever ... I swear! I seriously messed up agreeing to join Rooter on his hunt for that mysterious Sami vendor out in the middle of nowhere! We went over the first snowpeak and were barely two days into the north when some Blighted snowstorm "caught" us at the foot of the mountains! 
I will never forget the sight of an entire burdenbeast being swooshed away by the Blight-tainted snow. The only thing on my mind was: "Blasted blightery! I'm gonna die here..." 
But I guess I saved up enough luck for the past decade or so! The snowstorm was just meters away from us when an Ursus girl swooped in from the skies — I kid you not! She flew in on a snowboard, pulled off a perfect landing next to us, slammed a shield on the ground, and suddenly a wall of ice appeared with a WOOSH! That wall kept the snowstorm away! 
Anyway, she saved both Rooter and me. She escorted us into a cave that she called our temporary shelter. 
And there were all sorts of people there... Oathkeepers, Seš'qa caravan drivers, and even Landbreakers! 
I still remember how we just walked in and some tall and mean-looking Feline started pointing his finger at us, "These two! They're frauds plotting to bring their swindling ways to Sami! I heard all about their dirty plots along the way. Why are you saving these lowlives? Don't let them cheat you!" It was only then when I suddenly recognized the Feline. He was the guide who traveled with us a few days back... I had wondered why he suddenly returned the commission and refused to guide us any longer... 
Anyway, I thought we were finished but the Ursus lass who brought us in said nothing. She gave the Feline a glance and the big mean kittyboy ... just sat down and said nothing else. 
I wanted to thank her personally back then, but she soon rushed out to carry on with her SAR. I couldn't even get her name. Another SAR team soon arrived and brought us to safety. 
But I recognized the badges and markings on her outfit. They were the same as the illustrations in the shelter. 
What happened next...? Well, I found your place. That's what happened. Hah! Just take these supplies. My token of gratitude to that Ursus lass! 
— Recordings from a donor of the Northern Corridor Rescue Union </>

4886966315575262812:
<@profile.key>I still remember the first time I met Ekut. The patrolling Huntsingers brought her back to the camp and the girl was barely alive. It was difficult for us to fathom why such a young child was left to roam the wildlands for months on her own, a suffering that almost killed her with malnutrition and thirst. To treat the injuries on her right arm, we were forced to reconnect the nerves with circuitry. Of course, there is no need to be worried about the hazards posed by her right hand. We have designed a special pair of insulation gloves so that she may live normally like us. 
I named her Ekut, based on the strong and intelligent burdenbeast of our hanna. It is my hope that she could grow up strong, and survive the wind and frost like the powerful burdenbeast. She achieved all that, and went beyond my expectations. 
But Ekut grew up with an immense duty that seemed far too heavy for her. Perhaps it was her early victory over death itself, or her powerful name we took from the hanna. When she turned fifteen, Ekut started to seek the songs and roamed the wildlands. The Songseeker did everything she could to help villages and towns in dire straits, even when the challenges were far too great. 
I still remember that rainstorm. During our rescue operation, Ekut ventured alone into the heart of the tempest. We thought she would never come back. Just when all hopes seemed lost, an arc of lightning streaked across the sky. Like that arc, she pierced the fury of nature and leaped onto the last wagon. And with this act, the wildlands began to address her as Arclight. The name was woven into the hanna of numerous Songseekers. She took a name from the verses and returned a new name to their songs. 
I was not surprised when Arclight told me that she wanted to seek new answers from Endfield. In fact, it reminded me of the choice I made when I was younger. I once roamed far to the canopies of starry night, and from there I felt a chorus of hope and courage assembled in the floating city. 
Those memories felt like they belonged to a lifetime ago. But I believe that as long as the stars still shine in the sky, Civilization will one day be united once more. I also believe that Arclight, along with the names and oaths she wove into her hanna, will find the answers she truly seeks. 
— Letter of Recommendation for Endfield Industries, written by Aldertone Orisha</>

4929631835096281479:
<@profile.key>TRAINING LOG I 
ENVIRONMENT: Setting C 
GRAVITY: Setting 2 
SUBJECT: Gilberta 
RECORDER: Lil' Dodge 
Gilberta's training performance exceeded all benchmarks. For her Arts specialization, we modified the chamber with gravity field generators and tactical release nodes, anticipating a lengthy adjustment period. Yet her combat instincts proved sharper than projected: mission accomplished in 1/3 the estimated time, with notably restrained collateral damage (a rarity for these fragile systems, as you know). Her gravitational control hints at paradigm-shifting potential: a tactician who warps the battlefield itself, or perhaps a one-woman armada-breaker. With this being said, I'm sure every commander will vie for her deployment. 
Our next step is clear: help Gilberta unlock her full potential and refine her tactical instincts. Her abilities can turn the tide under any condition — and that's thrilling. 

TRAINING LOG VI 
ENVIRONMENT: Setting S
GRAVITY: Setting 26 
SUBJECT: Gilberta 
RECORDER: Lil' Dodge 
In recent training sessions, we've been exploring Gilberta's control over her Arts. When we increased the number of non-directional gravity sources to eight, her control over the surrounding field showed a marked decline. Most of the time, she was able to maintain stable gravity around herself, but at critical environmental thresholds, she could no longer stabilize areas a few meters away — which means she couldn't ensure the safety of nearby teammates. 
All relevant parameters and environmental conditions have been uploaded to EFDB. While Talos-II is unlikely to present situations with multiple gravity sources shifting unpredictably, the risks our operators face extend beyond this planet. At certain Depths, any battlefield condition is possible. 
Gilberta expressed some dissatisfaction with her performance, but she did more than enough. This training was designed specifically to test the limits of her abilities — and it's now our task to develop the next training phase accordingly.</>

5032822255139996211:
<@profile.key>[Clinical Diagnosis Report]
Imaging scans indicate blurred organ contours with observable abnormal shadows within the operator's body. Detection of Originium particles within the circulatory system shows anomalies, presenting signs of Oripathy. At this stage, the operator can be confirmed as an Oripathy-positive.

Attending Physician's Note: According to Tangtang's own account, she contracted Oripathy during a conflict between Qingbo Stockade and the Tianshi of Wuling City a decade ago. Tangtang stated she received no specialized treatment over these ten years, only various folk remedies provided sporadically by the stockade residents. However, examination results indicate that both her Cell-Originium Assimilation rate and Blood Originium-Crystal Density are remarkably well-controlled.
When questioned about this, Tangtang replied, "Everyone says I'm the River's Daughter. The water washes the sickness away."
Could natural bodies of water truly cure Oripathy? The answer is evident: it's impossible.

[Medication Application Form from a Tianshi]
Generic Medication Name: Oripathy Suppressant
Quantity Requested: Long-term supply
Infection Site of Patient: Ocular region
Reason for Application: Aiding Qingbo Stockade
Audio Log:
"Honestly, I'm a little hesitant about delivering the medication... The first time, he threw it right back at us, shouting, 'We won't take handouts from Wuling people!' and tried to charge at us with his head... Thankfully, the watchguards were there and helped us escape. I'm not afraid he'd kill me, it's just that we can't have another incident like that.
"The girl's Oripathy is on the verge of worsening. We're aware of it, and surely they are too. She contracted it because of us. We have an obligation to treat her.
"Back then ... it was sheer chaos. The sky was pitch black, low, and heavy with rain tearing through the curtain of clouds. We trudged through the howling wind as the stockade residents charged out from behind the hills, weapons in hand, their shadows in the night like malevolent spirits...
"People were terrified. No one noticed there was such a young child in the crowd...
"The rain washed away the blood... No, no, perhaps it never truly vanished. Whenever I close my eyes, I see that girl's wounded eye, and his ... the leader's eyes. The ones that said he just wanted to survive... I will never forget them for as long as I live.
"The second time, we left the syringes near the outskirts of Qingbo Stockade, where the residents were sure to pass by.
"This time, it wasn't charity from an enemy ... maybe it fell from the sky, grew from the earth, or was carried by a beast. Thinking of it that way might make it a little easier for them to accept.
"I waited two whole days ... and it was taken.
"I waited two more days and saw the girl playing by the water. She chased long-ears along the river for quite a distance. She seemed much better.
"I don't know what will happen from here on out ... and I don't think this counts as true atonement. But I had a full night's rest, at least for that day."</>

The judges and other tasters of this dish have, after lengthy deliberation and multiple rounds of discussion, arrived at a unanimous conclusion: This dish is the perfect symbol of the Endministrator's past memories. The presentation was unique. Though only mildly seasoned, the texture was compact and uniform, a package of energy and flavors compressed into ready-to-eat portions. A mysterious condiment was used, lending the rations flavors entirely new to us. If this condiment were made available on the market, it would add plenty of delightful culinary possibilities to Wuling.
Unfortunately, the works of other contestants were equally astonishing. This entry also tasted similar to standard military provisions, with the condiments being the only highlight.

5093319110565934174:
"I guess we're both trapped here. You and me. Now, we got a RAYTHEAN floor worker of their brand new production line and a stoic and well-grizzled UWST ... ughh ... What exactly is your job, bro?"
"Weapons. I install Originium primer safety mechanism on our guns."
"Grreeeat. That means we got an expert weaponsmith. Too bad the Blight blocked the roads and got us both trapped here. I'm gonna tell every Raytheanite about this once we get our sorry butts out of here... By the way, you're really good with bandages. I would never be able to fix my fractured finger if I were here alone."
"You should have picked up some first-aid knowledge. This is the wildlands, mate."
"It's my first time working out here. Frontier-fresh, you know. Heh. Never expected them to assign me to a field job but I guess they never got enough Raytheanites to work out here. We drew lots, and yeah, got myself the short end of the stick and ended up with the convoy escort job. Hey, I might not look like it, but I stashed away six months' worth of pay before leaving home. Bought myself three years' worth of general insurance and emergency first-aid coverage plans for the Band Periphery with a wad of cash this thick! LOOK! THIS THICK! And KABAM! Actual accident happens and none of them works! Wasted all my cold hard cash on this blightery... Hey. Why do you look so lost and confused?"
"Sorry... I ... just remembered a few things. I haven't seen printed creds for a while." 
"Oooh! That's right! I just remembered! The U-double-U don't use no cold hards. They pay you in ... what? Weekly ration tickets? Is that true?"
"We actually do get paychecks. In T-creds. But our sites tend to be in ... less populated regions of the frontier so we got nowhere to spend 'em. You might treat this job as a frontiers run, but it's more like a southward trip towards the safe zone for me. Our bases are also better stocked in these parts, so ... I guess I didn't really have to spend any money either way..."
"That sounds ... hmm ... boring ... I guess. Oh! Dang it! Nearly forgot about this! We get fat bonuses if we get good people into RAYTHEAN! So, interested in becoming a Raytheanite...? Wow. What did you just throw at me? Wait. This is a RAYTHEAN Pass... You gotta be joking. You're an ex-Raytheanite?"
"Yeah. Ex-Ray. They dechipped my pass when I left several years ago. I got to keep this as a souvenir."
"So why did you..."
"Because 'Innovation Makes Future' became Innovation Makes the Big Bucks. I just can't stand seeing new tech being used solely for profit. Look ... RAYTHEAN is a good company, make no mistake about that... Maybe it's just me who's weird... Anyways, we need to stick together to survive the frontiers. Things would be better if there were more Raytheanites out here, right?"
"You have a point, but... Wait. I'm hearing a ringing noise..."
"It's my comm device. My fellow crew mates must have noticed something wrong with my positioning signals. An SAR team should be on their way. We should try to get closer to the area's border."
"Wow. You're a true survivalist! So, uhm... You're really ... not thinking about rejoining RAYTHEAN? They may have bonuses for returning Ex-Rays... Being a Re-Ray might be..."
"Get moving already. Sheesh."

5107033768876830012:
<@profile.key>When Wulfgard first arrived at Endfield, his Landbreaker background raised a few eyebrows. Some operators suspected he had hidden motives. But as the years passed, they discovered that behind the cold exterior and sharp tongue was someone surprisingly easy to get along with. 
He's always punctual, efficient, responsible, and thorough with cleanup. In many ways, he checks every box of the "ideal teammate." Unfortunately, he doesn't seem to enjoy working with others. Whenever he's required to submit a report, he always requests to be assigned to solo operations instead. 
On the other hand, Wulfgard has never been shy about sharing his insights into weapon modifications, which has caused headaches for Endfield's Arsenal Engineers. Many operators first met Wulfgard during one of the regular new weapon testing sessions, where they'd watch him coolly tweak a weapon right in front of its designer, then say, "It works better this way." This often left the engineers on the verge of losing their composure. 
Though this earned him a spot on the arsenal team's "unwelcome" list, Wulfgard didn't care. He kept modifying every new weapon he got until it felt right to him. 

"Experienced hunters always select their best hunting tools or modify them until they become the best."</>

5149756073897471827:
<@profile.key>No Op Z7 teammates, except for Estella, have positive memories regarding the anniversary for the Founding of Operations Team Z7. However, everyone agrees that it was an unforgettable occasion. 
At first, Akekuri found that the team had no field assignments on the day of the anniversary and suggested celebrating the occasion. Everyone responded positively and a discussion on the means of celebration began. However, since the team was encamped in the wildlands far from the Civilization Band, every suggestion involved some form of outdoor activity. Estella, unwilling to engage in any physical exertion, naturally voted against every single proposal. In the end, "riverside picnic" was the only option that received a unanimous vote. It was not the favorite option for everyone, but at least nobody voted against it. 
On the day of the anniversary, everyone was eagerly gathering herbs and hunting game. Estella, however, returned with an armful of colorful mushrooms. Her teammates firmly rejected her suggestion to toss those wild fungi into the cooking pot. In the end, the team conceded. Under Captain Akekuri's watchful eyes, several unassuming mushrooms were allowed to be part of the stew. 
As the sun set, the team built a campfire by the river to enjoy what they had gathered throughout the day. Fortunately, the "unassuming" mushrooms turned out to be only mildly toxic. Unfortunately, they were incredibly spicy. The team emptied their water canteens to douse the heat but to no avail. Desperate, they rushed to the riverbank to wash the spicy taste away with river water. That's when they heard a low rumble coming from upstream — the dam had begun a reservoir drawdown! 
"Curses! I forgot about the UWST reservoir drawdown notice! That's why there's no field operations today!" 
No one remembered exactly how they ended up swept away by the floodwaters. When they finally came to, they found Estella skillfully surfing the rushing water on the team's cooking pot. The sun had already set when the four Op Z7 teammates pulled each other to shore. They had no choice but head downstream to look for Estella. Finally, the team found Estella sound asleep at a nomad campfire in wee hours of the morning. The nomads treated Akekuri and her team to a proper wildlands feast. 
The nomads were extremely accommodating and the team decided to rest there for the night. Just as Akekuri was about to fall asleep beneath the starry ceiling, she suddenly remembered a Z7 field assignment in a few hours' time. Estella could not be awakened, so the four Z7 teammates took turns carrying her. They successfully reached the Endfield camp at daybreak. 
According to Estella's memories, she was completely unconscious as she probably ate the other fungi she picked. She treated the flood waters as a packed and riotous moshpit, and fantasized herself as a rockstar performing a stage dive and surfing the crowd while she growled and screamed her songs. Nobody knew what Estella sang. Akekuri only remembered how she carried Estella and trudged back to camp. The young girl on her back did mumble a few things while she slept — things about home.</>

5201204394807128073:
Interested in the story of the messenger named Falken Gravning?
Hardly any Terran remembered his name. What they did remember was his job — a Catastrophe Messenger.
Falken was born in the borders of Kazimierz and started this lonesome career when he was but a young man. With sturdy steps he hiked across mountains and navigated rivers to bring verified data of Catastrophe signs to villages, towns, and cities facing inevitable destruction. He was not a savior. The only thing he could do was to warn people of the "impending footsteps of annihilation". Some were grateful, while others bore grudges. He suffered more than a few wounds, vile curses, as well as cold, hateful gaze over the years, and witnessed grave sacrifices as well. Suffocating depression constantly loomed over his soul.
When he turned 53, he decided to leave his homeland behind and devote the final years of his life to the newly discovered planet — Talos-II.
Here in the new world, he was no longer a "prophet of doom" but a mere common messenger. For those few short years, the serene, open expanses of Talos-II was free from Catastrophes and the ever-present threat of destruction. A sense of long-absent peace filled Falken. He took his backpack and roamed the lands between the pioneering bases, delivering letters, escorting supplies, and conveying intel with every trip.
When he had time for himself, he continued to travel alone. He even cobbled together several Originium devices to create a rudimentary aircraft. Though it flew awkwardly across the skies, he could land it upon unexplored wilderness. He climbed majestic peaks, waded through dark swamps, and left his footprints in this untouched realm.
He started to picture a beautiful future for the land untouched by Catastrophes — Once he retires, he would build a wooden cabin in some valley. He would also have a mountaintop summer retreat where he could enjoy clean, refreshing air. His notebook was filled with trails, routes, contour maps, and ideas for his "ideal home". As he spread footprints across the world, more and more of the blank areas were filled.
Until that very day.
The day when the Ankhors fell from the skies and the Aggeloi descended upon Civilization. People were once again forced to flee as they had done with Catastrophes struck.
During the era of chaos, Falken once again took up his old profession. He became the most reliable guide and led the survivors in a desperate southbound trek of survival. People said he stepped up to the challenge in numerous occasions to mark out the road forward. He even flew that ramshackle aircraft of his into the storm, and returned with a beaten and badly wounded body. With experience, grit, and courage, he paved multiple trails of life for the refugees. Numerous outposts, villages, and towns were founded thanks to his way markers or verbal directions. Without Falken as their guide, many would fail to reach the Civilization Band alive.
But that was not the end of Falken's journey.
During the last years of his life, Falken continued to journey south. He traversed the mountain ranges and roamed the entire Band until he finally arrived at the edge of the great ocean. Legend then claimed that Falken built a ship at that very place and sailed off to find a place even further south.
And Falken was never seen again after that.
A particular story was rather popular amongst the oceanic navigators of Talos-II: Should one see a cloud, shaped like a raised mast with full sails at the place where the oceans and heavens meet, that would be "Falken's Sail", and that would be tidings for good weather.
People call him the "Navigator". Falken's story was told in every city, school, and port. He was a messenger, a pathfinder, a lonely traveler, and the one who raised the beacon high during humanity's hour of darkness.
Mieszko Industries is proud to uphold this concept. Our latest series for this year have been imbued with the spirit of the Navigator. We dedicate it to every ambitious and courageous individual eager to face the boundless future.

"This is just a promotional tale. Is this all I'm going to read?"
"Of course not. I'm about to introduce this latest paper on the untold secrets of Falken Gravning."
"Oh?"
"Falken Gravning kept going south because of his belief that another undiscovered Cosmic Gate may exist in the south pole of this world."
"Another ... Cosmic Gate? Is that even possible?"
"Who knows? Nobody could prove nor disprove it. Who knows? He might have found a way home at the end of the sea, or simply disappeared beneath the waves."
"So..."
"So towards the end of his life, what kept the 'Navigator' going wasn't his passion for exploration or adventure. It was a far more deeper desire — He wanted to go home."

5266813208237621230:
<@profile.key>The Oathkeepers have twenty words for rain. When I saw her again, it felt as though she had been smothered and battered by each and every type of the downpours. 
"Azrila," I said, "We all make mistakes." 
Those were feeble words. I had never served in any assault banner, and I was not at the frontlines. Like the others, I still had zero knowledge how she survived the living hell. I had no intention of paying any heed to the festering rumors — doubting your fellow Oathkeeper was a path to calamity. Even if the concerns were grounded in reasonable doubt, any fracture should never emerge from within. 
She remained silent and swept the surroundings with her stiff gaze. No one else came to see her off. This was the first time when I failed to read her expressions. It was not sadness, but neither was it release. I thought I could hear her breathe a sigh of relief. 
"The Castellan has given his verdict." I said. 
I wanted to give her a pat on the shoulder but quickly realized it wasn't the right thing to do. For the first time, her pauldrons seemed too big for her, and the straps rattled against her dagger. 
There was no way we could fit the cremation urns of twenty-eight Oathkeepers into her baggage, no matter how hard we tried. Azrila settled on something else, insisting on carrying their service tags with her at all times. They clattered and rang with every movement, but no one laughed. 
"Nilla's weapon has been placed in the Vault of Relics. Her family will be here in a few days to retrieve her other things. Don't you want to go and see them again?" 
"No," she finally spoke, "I intend to set off at once." 
Nilla's family placed great importance on traditions, and turned down the Order's request of returning the cremation urns and her personal effects. 
Perhaps I should not question any further. My neck slightly itched. Perhaps several pairs of eyes were gazing at this place from behind the safety of the windows and blinds that demarcated the boundaries. 
"Go forth and see to our mission." I opened the gates of the keep. The vehicle was already loaded and had been waiting for a while. The driver was meticulously cleaning the mud from the bumpers as it always rained in the north. "I shall look after your patron firearm. I swear it by my name."  
"Gratitude, Grand Treasurer." Azrila then paused for a while. 
"The Crusade will eventually end." </>

Outsiders may regard this branch as the military arm of the Cabal of Tranquility as it is full of clerics clad in heavy armor and wielding heavy guns and cannons. The Resurgonites are charged with the defense of Cabal temples. They also actively take part in the excavation of Precursor ruins. Although they claim their goal is to "restore the Holy City", not every action is an act of faith. Some simply enjoy this way of life.
5267207189668876502:
I received Walther's specifications. Absolutely ludicrous! I had to double-check with the man because I suspect he wrote an extra zero for the desired yield. Mister blightbrain wasn't asking for an Arts Unit. He was asking for a portable giga-cannon. The nutcase claimed that he desperately needed the weapon to push our frontiers further into the wildlands. Even if I were to successfully make this mother-of-all-guns, I balk at the idea of handing over this dangerous weapon of mass devastation to the frontiersfolk.
......
Theoretically speaking, the yield was ... feasible. My calculations placed it within achievable limits but the numbers look risky. I had to redesign the structure of the Arts Unit but this would ... double the weight of the entire setup.
......
A powered eviscerator with a moving chain of cutting bits? Their demands are getting nuttier with every single day. Are they really going to smash an enemy's head with this Arts Unit? Come to think about it, the current build does have enough room for a motor. Maybe I'll give it a shot.
......
They pushed further into the front and Walther invited me for a field tour. To quote the man, he wanted to see what my works had "achieved". It would be nice if mister blightbrain could explain how my prototype vanished and how they failed to salvage anything. Not even a piece of scrap.
......
It was a monumental sight that I'd remember for life. The Aggelos was as big as a mountain and its carcass blocked the entire valley. Walther told me how he remotely controlled the prototype to ram against the Aggelos and how the mounted power eviscerator created an opening on its crust. The explosive payload was then detonated with ... ground shattering effects.

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrator Authority Level ≥ 3</>
<@gray.note>· Complete main mission [Chapter Ⅰ Process Ⅰ: Valley Reboot]</>
<@gray.note>· Not logged into the game for ≥ 21 consecutive days</>
<@gray.note>· Participation in the last [Protocol Reconnection] event ≥ 42 days ago</>
<@gray.note>· Duration of the Endministrator's Return status is the same as the event period of the [Protocol Reconnection] event</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· Visiting the [Protocol Reconnection] event page for the first time gives a Protocol Reconnection reward, Endministrator.</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, visit the [Protocol Reconnection] page and earn rewards by taking part in [Reconnection Sign-In] and [Reconnection Tasks].</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, perform sign-ins at [Reconnection Sign-In] every day to earn rewards. Daily sign-ins can be performed for 7 times.</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, complete various [Reconnection Tasks] to earn event points and rewards. Earning enough points also gives extra rewards.</>
<@gray.note>Data tallying for [Reconnection Tasks] may experience slight delays. After completing task requirements, please wait 2-5 minutes for the data to synchronize.</>
<@gray.note>· All event rewards will be sent to your in-game Mail. Reward mail will expire in 30 days so claim them on time, Endministrator.</>
<@gray.note>· The event will end 14 days after your participation and close at 4:00 AM (server time).</>

5667242983892543269:
<@profile.key>In the beginning, children at the Den of the Brave always poked fun at Adelheid. No one really knew the girl who lived so far away. She was shy, introverted, dressed in boring clothing, slow to react, and never caught the ball during conversations. "Biggie" Tom started by badmouthing her at every turn. He wanted her out of the children's secret base. But Tom eventually relented because of two reasons: The grown-ups respected the Caprinae couple who adopted Adelheid, and even though she was teary eyed and sniveling, she never forgot about stuffing herself with apple peelings and turnip leaves. 

Everything changed in that atrocious summer. The rains poured in June, the entire place was flooded, and the bandits were finally evicted after a grueling battle. The ordeal left scars upon five families who lost their loved ones. The unsuspecting children followed the procession to the suburbs, and they saw her atop the hills. Adelheid, except it was not the little crybaby they knew. They saw the girl and her family cleaning out the resting places, cleaning the headstones, and offering elegies to the deceased. It was not until the coffin was laid to rest when the children finally realized that Tom would no longer return to Den of the Brave. 
Instead of fear, it was panic and denial that spread. The children could not accept the machinations of fate, and failed to figure out a way to make up for the missing position. It was then when Adelheid placed a simple wooden statuette before Tom's grave. It was very roughly carved, crooked, imbalanced, and unpolished. 
"He always wanted a figurine of a Competition Knight," the girl said. "That would be Tom's final wish." 
The grown-ups fail to control their tears again. They lamented the death of a life so innocent and young. 
But the children tried not to laugh as the statuette looked nothing like a Competition Knight. 
In the end, they bore it no longer and burst out laughing. 
They took out their Knight Cards and discussed how to make the statuette more knight-like. Although Adelheid failed to follow their conversation and the task of carving the figurine fell to a more dexterous companion, the children would always hand the new and much improved statuette into Adelheid's hands, for she was tasked with leading their own procession into the cemetery and perform the annual ceremony. 
"Tom is right here," the girl promised every year. 
No one knew how Adelheid was so certain about this, or how she learned of Tom's wishes. Nobody seemed to care about how she performed her work. 

In the end, Adelheid did not break her shell. She remained rather inept at expressing herself. Every time her tummy rumbled, she became so embarrassed she would start inching to a nearby dustbin. But whenever new kids arrive in town and start talking about the strange girl humming songs in the bushes, someone would tap them on the shoulder and give them a quite a story.</>

5697461984948013663: Unknown to most, the purifying powers of the Ancestral Spring were not a miracle. They were actually achieved by the presence of a Yanese irrigation machine located at the bottom of the Ancestral Spring. This machine was installed over a century ago by Yanese Tianshi, and it kept the water clean by separating the Blight from the flowing aquifer. Given its age and lack of maintenance, the Blight buildup within the machine was close to reaching critical levels and posed a risk to Qingbo Stockade located at the ground level above. In the end, a knowledgeable Stockade resident sincerely asked the Endministrator to seal the machine with Originium Arts, thus ending the extended and exhausting operational run of the machine.

5706890293250190000:
<@profile.key>Reconvener and Operator Laevatain joined Endfield with the Endministrator's direct recommendation. After acquiring her personal agreement, as well as approval from the HR Division and authentication from Rhodes Island, Laevatain joined Endfield as an operator under the direct command of the Endministrator. 

Laevatain arrived at the reception area a full 45 minutes ahead of the agreed-upon schedule. She simply placed the employment notification on the table and said little else except, "I'm here. We can begin." 
She was quite cooperative during the initial phase of the interview — giving her signature, submitting a retinal scan, and linking her Protocol Authority Level without a single fuss or comment. I tried to liven up the atmosphere by saying, "Welcome to Endfield." She nodded and replied, "I'm already here." 
During job-inclination testing, I followed the prescribed list of questions and asked if she considered utilizing her skill set in other non-combat roles, such as combat planner, coach, or instructor. She did not say anything but appeared obviously annoyed. 
In the end, Operator Laevatain left with this final line: "Just let me go to as many places you can — places that let me build as much memories as I can." 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

5762123140210423383:
The man walked in and quickly slammed the door of the simple barracks. The recalcitrant door barely managed a painful creak before it was forcibly shut to block the frigid air outside. The man then looked around and breathed a long sigh of relief. His daughter was still asleep in her cubicle. The cub of the Pack was four, and the hardest to coddle to sleep.
His wife prepared the weapons. The scope and Arts Unit had been taken apart, the parts laid out across the table. She was wholly focused on honing a short blade when her man walked in.
"Cleaning up the weapons rack?" The man sat on the opposite side of the table and pried open a preheated tin of meat provisions. The temperature was just right and appetizing enough.
"I hardly make the wrong guesses, dear. You'll be using these soon enough, right?"
"I actually wanted to play a prank on you and tell you that: 'My sweet darling, your judgment about the Hunt Meet was completely off.' Unfortunately, the Old Wolves said those very same words, as though they read a secret script you prepared for them... So yes, I lost the bet and so I'll coddle our pup to sleep for a week... But just so you know, I don't like losing."
The woman threw the sharpened blade to her man and gestured him to try the balance. She then picked up a gun to load it up with ammo.
"The Pack never shared Jakub's views. The Landbreaker clans will definitely break apart... And if we're no longer 'friends', we can only be this — predator and prey... The clan must hunt them down and feast upon the kill. We'll become clannibals."
"Clannibals... I like that word. Old Louis shared that view in the meeting: 'The Civilization Band is closing its grip around the Landbreakers' neck. The Wolves cannot die like this. We must see if our friends are good enough for the table...' Hah! That old geezer has a way with words."
"So which Clan will it be?"
"Bonekrushers. They like Jakub too much. These fanatics kinda disgust me."
"And the hit?"
"A denstack smash-and-run, maybe? Grab their supplies tonight and then head home right afterwards. Nobody would know that the Pack did it. If I set off in half an hour, I can even make it back for breakfast."
"Just don't forget to say goodbye to our baby."
"...We probably shouldn't wake her."
"You sure?"
"I'm sure. The hit is just a stroll in the park..."
*BAMMM*.
The man saw his woman slam the gun on the table and coldly glared at him. An unloaded bullet rolled across the table, fell off the corner, clinked on the floor, and continued its journey to the corner of the barracks. He knew his wife saw through his lies.
"Don't lie to me."
"I never..."
"Guessing the details of your meeting wasn't hard... Jakub's negotiations with the Civilization Band fell apart. He and his loyalists will be torn apart... There's only one thing you want ... to keep the Pack safe in these turbulent times." The woman paused and breathed deeply before continuing. "Wolves will be killing Wolves. The Clan will be forced to do housekeeping. Those who stand with Jakub will be eliminated. Even the Wolves who made their 'names' in the Civilization Band by raiding and killing will be bled dry and left to die in the wildlands. And that isn't the end of it. Some of you will be sent out to war with the other Clans. Direct, open war. It's a sign. The Banders know the best time for their offensive approaches. They know the Pack takes a different path compared to the other Clans... You want to rush the end of the Landbreakers... And some of you volunteered for this suicidal path so that the Clan can survive."
"How did you guess?"
"Because my tally is higher than yours, dear. I see murder glistening in your eyes."
"Love, I do not fear this... Maybe when I close my eyes, I'll see Nonno waiting at our old villa in Siracusa... I'll tell him that I took care of my beloved wife, my baby girl, and all the other cubs... I'll tell him that our legacy lives on, running atop the grazing fields, counting creds in a city alley, or even embracing new lives as Talosians... Our Famiglia name will be cleansed anew with the name of our Clan... No matter the life we chose... the Pack will decide for ourselves..."
"I'll go with you."
"I forbid it. I chose to join this Hunt and accept death so that you may live. And you must live, even if you have to forget about our homes and abandon the Famiglia. I..."
The man could not go on. The girl's little palm opened to reveal the bullet that rolled off to the corner.
"Good hunting, Papa..."
His girl just woke up and heard only part of the conversation, but that was enough. He originally planned to leave without goodbye, and had to armor his resolve by taking a long walk in the blistering cold. But those soft words of encouragement shattered it and jerked him out from the great river called Death.
"I..."
"We are bound by blood, and that is how legacy is carried out, isn't it?"
The soft hands, small hands, and rough hands gripped each other.
"Before the cubs could freely roam the wilderness, no one can kill us. No one."

5781618409543038911:
I once had extreme confidence.
Confidence for my gifts in wielding the (applicable) Originium Arts. And my gifts were rare indeed, even when taking every caster of Talos-II into account.
I believe all of my Arts instructors I had since my childhood could attest to this fact, and there were 23 of them.
While my peers struggled to light a candle with an Arts Unit, I was debating the possibility of having an unaided Infected cast the Arts for extended periods time and at higher throughputs... It was a time when I first heard about the legendary Endministrator. Back then, at least, I once believed that I might be capable of achieving the same.
My performances in the Originium Arts Proficiency Assessment for the last 20 years, my GPA from the courses on Originium Arts theories during the academy, and the accolades I received for my graduation thesis titled The Gap Between Practical Applications and Theory — A Discourse on the Future of the Originium Arts, only served to strengthen my belief as I spent every year perfecting my Arts. There is a significant possibility that I am a "genius".
— And I held on to that belief until I joined the company that I have dreamed about since my childhood. Endfield Industries.

I could hardly describe my feelings when I underwent the routine integrated physical examinations for new employees and received the report.
— My Originium Arts Assimilation was rated as "Standard". How was it possible? They must have made a mistake.
At that moment, I felt as though I was struck by the devious Arts of a Djall, and it struck so hard I could fight off the mental fog.
When I came to my senses, I went straight to the Human Resources Division and delivered a severe protest. I demanded a re-examination and declared my absolute refusal to accept such a brusque conclusion made without sufficient evidence.
The HR Division seemed to be expecting my applications. I was entreated by Martin Marvin Malen in a most sincere manner.
"...We understand your requests and have arranged two additional Originium Arts Assimilation testing sessions after three working days. A total of two operators will also be assigned to take the tests with you and serve as control references."

Three days later —
As per the instructions, I cut out a perfect square out of a ferric slab before me with my Arts Unit. I turned around saw the Savra who should be at the opposing testing station. He looked at me curiously.
"Is this the way to do it? I thought I had to cut 16 squares with a single move!"
This is my confession. I thought I heard him wrongly and subconsciously gazed at the results of his test... And I saw sixteen perfect squares neatly laid out on the floor of the training cabin. The clock stopped at exactly 1 minute and 1 second.
— What was I doing at that time? Oh, I just finished calibrating my Arts Unit and thought about the perfect approach to start cutting the slab.

There was no question that I lost the first trial. It was a complete disaster.
But negativity had yet to catch up to me. I simply prepped for the second session. I comforted myself with this thought: "Endfield's a big place, and they have a legend like the Endministrator. A fellow Endfielder of such competence ... should be something to expect."
Soon, an accident, or the stroke of Fate ... dawned upon me.
While I was checking the Arts Unit, an Ægir youth wearing an elite operator's uniform poked his head out of the training cabin. He simply wagged his tail and undid the safeties of over fifty standard Arts Units. They orbited him like an obedient school of fins as he left the place and mumbled these words: "What a hassle... Shouldn't have come here at the first place..."
This was when I first experienced despair from seeing an "unattainable gap" that could never be closed.
I learned that even if I mastered every form of Originium Arts to my knowledge, the tracelessness and effortless elegance of his Arts wielding were qualities that I could never achieve...
Never, not in my entire lifetime.
I suddenly felt that all my achievements in the Arts for the last twenty years only gave me better knowledge of what I witnessed at a particular moment in life — I am not the prodigy. That title belongs to someone else.
I chose to forfeit the second testing session.

After the test, I also gave up joining the Specialist Tech Division. Thankfully, the Arsenal took a good look at my CV and asked if I was interested in doing some weapon engineering.
I agreed.

They said that the classic "Arts Identifier" would be returning for the Arts Unit Obstacle Challenge this year — I received five of those, but I would not mind getting a sixth.

5795012384432655302:
"Talos will become an incredible city," pa and ma said, "Andre Zlotareed, be a good apprentice, learn the chores, and attend a proper trades school. Once you're a big owl, you gotta leave this tiny village, find a good job at the largest Band city of Talos-II, start a family, and enjoy your happy, cozy life."
"Talos," Andre read his textbook, "is the gas giant that Talos-II orbits around. During the few years when the Cosmic Gate was still open, the famous scientist Ferdinand Clooney calculated the mass of the planet as well as the orbital radius of Talos-II. The Starpod is seemingly absent on Talos-II, allowing astronomers to create star maps far more extensive than those they made by studying the skies of Terra."
"The stars are pretty close, but the Cosmic Gate has already closed," Andre's mentor told him. "If you want to learn something more besides applied Originium sciences, you can attend the astrologer's salon in the Academy. Divination is not the salon's only focus. They're also interested in astronomy. Scientific astronomy, that is. But I think your interest in the stars goes beyond observations and records, Andre. You want to build a star, right?"
"The stars are beyond your reach," the dealers and proxies of violence were always an arrogant lot.
"No. The stars are in my hand," Andre answered, "Gentlemen, allow me to introduce ... the very first Arts Unit prototype that I've designed for Endfield Industries." The Arts Unit perched on his shoulder leaped into the air and gave off a burst of light so bright that it resembled a hypernova in the deepest recesses of space.

5891198471955243046: Later discoveries revealed that every individual possesses an innate affinity for specific types of Originium Arts and their mastery levels, a phenomenon termed "Originium Art Uniqueness". This inherent nature demonstrates that human capacity for channeling these arts varies fundamentally from birth. To bridge these natural disparities and harness the benefits of Originium Arts more efficiently, humanity developed technology to encapsulate these arts within crystalline units, releasing them through mechanical systems. These innovations, known as "Encapsulated Casting Units", transformed Originium Arts into standardized technology and paved the way for the explosive growth of Originium industry. 5931956550209320020: <@profile.key>Seven years ago, during the autumn when I left Juniper Security, I worked as a part-time instructor at a youth training camp somewhere close to home. I was hoping to take care of my sick father and make some money for his treatment. My main job description was to share various tidbits about military basics with children who had probably never set foot in the Band Fringes. I worked in the youth camp for three years, and a 15-year old student named Ruy left quite an impression on me. He was the only one in the cohort who signed up temporarily. They told me his parents did not want him to join, but conceded upon the boy's strong insistence. I often reflect upon this moment and think I should have taken this as a warning sign for things to come. Despite being so thin that he looked malnourished, Ruy was not as obedient as he appeared. Ruy only attended eleven courses throughout the entire term, and he "found problems" in eight of them. These problems ranged from slight deviations from textbook examples of military poise to one or two minor memory slips. In fact, he pointed out any non-conformances in my course that could be considered errors. Things escalated to the point where I had to rehearse my materials before every class just so the boy wouldn't call out my mistakes. On the eve of graduation, I finally eased off my usual sternness as an instructor and asked the boy a question that had troubled me for months, "I need to know why you're so obsessed about finding your instructor's mistakes? Did I make you angry at some point?" I still remember the expression on his face. He shook his head, and then took out a copy of How to Be a Proper Soldier? (Young Adults Edition) published by the TGCC. The manual was flipped through so many times that the corners curled. Then he pointed the book at me and gave me a serious look. "No, Instructor Luca. Your classes were very interesting, and your examples made things way more engaging than the manual. I actually liked your lessons a lot... But you probably didn't know that everything in this manual was the only fount of strength that's kept me this far." "I ... I really care about every pointer in the manual because I want to put them to use on a real battlefield... I've been dreaming about this since I was nine..." After hearing these words, I finally learned about Ruy's childhood from another instructor and a close friend of mine — turned out that young Ruy contracted Oripathy due to momentary familial negligence. Though timely treatment was provided, the poor lad was almost forced to live out the rest of his life on a sickbed. I do not know what he experienced or sacrificed in order to receive training like the other students... But as his instructor, I do approve of his efforts and proudly call him a student of mine, despite the fact that many of his actions had caused no small trouble on my part. Come to think of it, if not for Ruy's constant, incessant, and persistent corrections, I would never have worked so hard to ensure conformance to the training materials. I probably would never correct my habits of mindless and rote memorization back in the force. And I most certainly would never have made it into the elite armed security training program or become an formal instructor for a mercenary outfit. ............ After my father passed away, I left Alloycarrot and drifted away from my training camp colleagues... I did not know where Ruy went afterwards. An accidental encounter with a former student of mine informed me that Ruy seemed to have joined a relatively famous company as part of their security detail... Truth be told, I was a little surprised. I thought that boy would have become an officer or instructor... Regardless, I sincerely hope he will make good use of the knowledge he acquired and fulfill his childhood dreams. — An excerpt from Those Not Remembered, an autobiography by the legendary instructor Luca Londi

5932676869511874060:
The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: The chef who prepared this plate of bamboo sprouts and meat knew perfect heat control! The young bamboo sprouts were sweet and crunchy, while the fillet slices were juicy and tender. Quick stir-frying over strong fire perfectly combined the two. The sprouts soaked up the rich gravy as the fillet slices absorbed the refreshing aromas of the bamboo grove.
But everything after the stir-frying revealed the chef's shortcomings. Too many condiments were used. The aroma of redjade ginseng also overpowered the flavors of the primary ingredients. The judges really had to work their palates to peel away the distractions before finally arriving at the refreshing tastes of nature and cozy family stoves. In conclusion, this dish felt like a dime a dozen.

6008543993566877738:
Operator Arcane (given name: Li Zhiyan) is a Hongshan Academy of Sciences Yinglung Special Task Force (YSTF) captain. Following injuries suffered during an operation in the North Wuling Exclusion Zone (EZ), she has been permitted to take an extended R&R. With special permission from the YSTF, Arcane is now a partner of Endfield Industries. 

According to Operator Arcane herself, this is her first long R&R. Our medical operators identified several dozen sites of internal injuries. Frontline work in dangerous Blight environments did not destroy her spirit, yet it has taken a toll on her in the form of constant and grueling physical pain. After conversing with members of the HAS, we learned that the HAS hopes that she can use the opportunity for proper recovery. "It should be a break to make up for all the breaks she missed for several years running." 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries

6028680177338019446:
<@profile.key>"The thing is, those towns and villages have been completely abandoned. According to the path described by Fluorite herself, you'll realize that she's like a scavenger, swooping from one dying town to the next. Once a new 'corpse' appears, she would spread her wings and 'fly off' again..." 
"I'm not questioning her motives. These vagabonds were not the ones who caused the towns to fade away. Folks could only find less than ideal places to settle, places that only had limited access to support and resources from the fringes. If a hub city meets a crisis, then the resulting supply breakdown could initiate a chain reaction capable of killing every town along the route." 
"Death starts from the most isolated spots, the weakest link of the chain. Refugees would then ride the wave of collapse until they either arrive at a new town that offers enough stability, or fade away completely in the wildlands like a certain town out there... People even came up with a special name to describe this phenomenon." 
"As long as the links between our cities remain fragile and tenuous, the stranding will never end. Crisis breeds privilege, and privilege is a hot bed for sin. Once we reach the threshold, Civilization is no longer a home, but a yoke that the monopolists shackle onto the necks of their slaves." 
"We must master the power that can help us prevent this from happening." </>

6036951869095039386:
<@profile.key>A massive Landbreaker army had encircled a wildlands village. The breakers prepared themselves for a full scale assault. Hopelessness spread like a plague amongst the garrisoned Endfield operators and the local residents. 
That was the moment when they saw her. Arclight of the Hannabit Circuit — the lone Songseeker who roared into the town astride her motorcycle. 
She tore through the rear of the breaker army and seized control of the situation without hesitation. Under her leadership, the residents created a defense line with their wagons and salvage. The elderly and children were moved to the hills. Everyone who could work dug the trenches and set up simple Originium defense units. Every action was calm and simple, and she moved throughout the place like a part of the wildlands itself. 
On the eve of the Landbreaker attack, thunder rumbled from afar. The monsoons came as usual, and a nightlong of torrential rain carved a new creek that coursed through the place. Arclight may have memorized every corner of the wildlands. The river coursed through the trenches that Arclight directed and became a protective moat that protected the entire village. The flood waters also washed through the breakers' camp and foiled their attack formations. 
The Landbreakers did not leave empty handed. They attempted to ford the moat and assail the defenses, but every attack was beaten back by Arclight, the Endfielders, and village residents. In the end, the raiders were forced to escape under the cover of a rainy night. 
When the dark rainclouds finally left the wildlands, Arclight found the Endfield operator who was sending a report to Dijiang. She waved a letter of recommendation to the skies and said, "The quaking of the wildlands dirt led me here. Now, please lead me to Endfield."</>

6186200711186841241:
She saw it more clearly than anyone else.
Stepping through the gate was a journey that was a tad quicker than she had imagined. Her eyes saw illusions and indescribable colors while her skin felt unreal sensations brushing against it. There were flashes of dizziness, bursts of murmurings, and fragments from the past. These were quickly followed by a bitter cold of winter trailing into something even more frigid. Everything felt so real to her.
She immediately asked everyone around and they gave her different answers. Some said it was over in a blink of an eye. Others panted with exhaustion and claimed that they were trapped in a stasis for days.
She was curious about the things she saw, for curiosity was part of her nature. She spent hours memorizing the patches, lines, and irregular hues of color that covered them. Yet all of them remained ephemeral and meaningless like the whimsical daydreams of spring.
Then she saw a gossamer-like beam and a clear glimpse of the starry skies unhindered by the Barrier.
She witnessed Talos.

Time did not bring sunrise to the polar regions of Talos-II. The huge, new world dominated everyone's view and it gripped her soul with a power that far exceeded the Arts of the Witch King himself. She could not avert her gaze. She could not have imagined how this natural-occurring astronomical entity enraptured her just as knowledge and wealth had seized her every attention. In fact, she was not sure if this view of the Universe could be classified as a natural phenomenon.
She took her time to inspect Talos. It was a massive yet almost motionless orb veiled in pale blue and decorated with rings that resembled the fringes of petals, like lovely ribbons that she only saw from her aunt's sewing machine. As she looked on, she realized that the "motionlessness" of Talos was a visual trick it played on her. It moved at an almost imperceivable pace and shimmered ever so gently. She could hear the wind.
However, the motions stopped quickly and Talos became an unmoving giant once more. Her heart raced as irregular explosions of thoughts, memories, and ideas surged through her mind. She remembered handing in her first Arts exam when she was still a girl and the moment when she completed her advanced studies at the Spire. She saw her parents, her mentor, the hand that reached out from the devastated ruins, and the flowing sands in the far distance. The last things she saw as she steadied her gait by the dark coastline were her own face, the skies of Leithanien, and childhood friends who counted the stars with her. All of them wore her own face.
For the first time in her life, she felt the powerful impulse of passionate love. Her heart yearned for Talos and she would do anything to touch the giant.
It was her belief that the giant celestial body was the knot that tied everything together.
And these ties made the Originium flowing within her whisper into a chorus.
That was the day when she stopped changing. She must become a long-lived one, like the world she had hopelessly fallen in love with.

6191673157022112314: Thanks to the data you have uploaded, we can now make the following deductions: The Yinglung Special Task Force (YSTF) is wholly responsible for the defenses of Yinglung Pass. However, the YSTF will require support from other personnel on matters that lie outside their defensive duties. Starting from this conclusion, we perused vast amounts of data and found out that the Hongshan Academy of Sciences (HAS) has, where necessary, authorized short-term residency for laborers or Tianshi who passed their background checks. You may have seen several Tianshi conducting research in this area to handle the threat posed by the Very Large Rift (VLR). Although the North Wuling Exclusion Zone (NWEZ) is more fitting for field research compared to Yinglung Pass, entering the NWEZ would be considered a suicidal act for most people. Yinglungs who periodically venture deep into the NWEZ could not carry many scientific instruments for safety considerations. Hence, the Tianshi were forced to conduct their scientific research in the relative safety of Yinglung Pass. Furthermore, the duration of their residency and the area of their field studies must undergo multiple rounds of review by HAS Headquarters. Any scientific process or task can only be conducted while accompanied by the members of the YSTF.

After countless attempts and sacrifices, following the reactivation of the precursor celestial calculation and measurement device known as the "Celestial Fulcrum" within Terra's home star system, humanity finally identified the Cosmic Gate's paired counterpart. <@profile.key>DOCUMENT: A prayer slip, kept within a book 
O merciful Lord, I praise thee. 
Grant language onto me, but lead me not into voicing it with vulgar words. 
Grant intellect onto me, but lead me not into employing it for blasphemy. 
Grant benevolence onto me, but lead me not into abusing it with arrogance. 
Grant vision onto me, but lead me not into corrupting it with base and common images. 
Grant friendship onto me, but lead me not into terminating it with violence. 
Grant eternal curiosity onto me, but lead me not into wasting it with ignorance. 
(NOTE: After verification, prayers of every Cabalist are based on their most truthful desires. The Cabal of Tranquility encourages their members to compose their own prayers. The followers of the Lord have their own belief and will about the Lord. And that is how they define their faith.) 

DOCUMENT: Excerpt from a diary 
Four years have passed since I boarded this vessel. The task of repair is progressing slowly, but with efficacy. Although the undertaking has become relatively uneventful, I have gradually found my way to return to my inner calm. This is the place where I can work in a dark and cold cabin. The rhythms of machines offer me incredible comfort. This is where I need not worry whether my actions would bring trouble to the others. Of course, this is also where I can share fragmented conversations with data residues of the Originium Network. In the Network, I experienced never-seen-before ruins of splendorous majesty that Sankta share their empathy. I heard the lamentations from myriad souls of the Sarkaz erased from existence. I witnessed the incessant bleeding of rebellion and subservience. And every time I wanted to take another step, these experiences would suddenly cease. The silence is frightening. I continued my calculations and repairs, hoping I can hear it speak once more. 
............ 
Something I feared even more has happened. I realized that the Originium has been polluted, damaged, and obliterated. It seems intertwined with what I perused in the various tomes of the Cabal. But why is this the case? What does Originium mean with regards to the codes of civilization that we are deciphering? And what is the entity capable of polluting, damaging, and even obliterating the Originium? 
Perhaps this is why Abbess Mumu sent me here. 
............ 
That was the day when I noticed a strange glow on Supervisor Perlica's visage. But this glow was quickly extinguished by her constant pondering. They are whispering. Something big is about to happen. Will it be relevant to the answers I seek? 
............ 
Black hair, black mask, and black shadow. Operator Chen Qianyu introduced the Endministrator to me. The one who had been hibernating in the medical bay. 
And for an instant, I seemed to achieve a form of empathy. A cacophony and impulse for joy, laughter, and tears exploded within me. I could not restrain the shaking of my material form. This entity is the answer, but I have not prepared my questions. </>

The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: A wondrous plate of Wuling fried rice, its perfect amber rice grains shimmered like gold and brimming with Wok Hey! At first bite, we were misled into thinking that it was just another serving of garden fried rice — until the rich flavors of hearty meat suddenly exploded in our mouths.
More work could be put into the ingredients and processing. The meat cubes were juicy indeed, but they failed to perfectly complement the rice. A single helping was enough to ignite greasy heartburn. Nevertheless, we consider this first-rate culinary work when compared to the other submissions.

The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: This plate of garden stir-fry was truly creative. The young bamboo sprouts were crunchy, refreshing, and sweet; the powdered yazhen was smooth and soothing; and the redjade ginseng slices unleashed their unique aromas after a quick pass through the heated wok.
Unfortunately, this would have been perfect if the dish were titled "Season's Produce, Simply Skilleted". Keeping the three primary ingredients aromatically distinct was no easy feat, but the product lacked the rich yet refreshing aroma required of a proper Garden Stir-Fry. What a pity.

<@profile.key>DOCUMENT: Alesh's Daily Routine 
06:00: Wakes up. Prepares angling gear and bait. Sharpens the hooks while mumbling to himself: "Gotta really hook these fins today." 
06:30: Sets off. Throws some fin bones to the cloudbeast strays in the streets. 
06:45-09:15: Morning angling time. Finds a place where water flow is slower. Waits patiently for the fins. 
09:15-10:00: Wraps up the angling gear. Checks his catch. Inspects the conditions of the caught fins. "Special" catches are set aside for other uses. 
10:00-12:00: Fin stall morning shift. Fins are arranged neatly in a specific number of rows and directions. These details matter a lot. 
12:00-13:00: Lunch time. Roasted fin over fire. Eats while glancing at his handyterm. 
13:00-15:00: Takes a nap or walk. Sometimes goes off to the wharf to review his angling skills, or sits on the bench to talk to strangers on proper angling spots. Jabbers on and on while making the fins restless in the water. 
15:00-17:30: Fin stall afternoon shift. Continues selling fins while observing the passers by for any "familiar faces" that shouldn't be showing up. 
17:45-19:45: Night angling at deeper waters. Final push during sunset. Specifically targets "great fins" that tend to be cautious and alert. These specimens are most active during sunset. This is also the time when enemies are most likely to make their mistakes. 
20:00-21:00: Dinner time. Organizes his catch for the day. Records the angling results and daily "fintel" he gathered from all that talking. 
21:00-22:00: Rest time. Relaxes on the balcony and gazes at the stars with his only eye. Occasionally gets reminded of those eventful nights... The turf wars are distant memories, yet they also felt like undercurrents that could suddenly snag him while he angles. 
22:00: Prepares the bait. Checks his angling gear. "Who'll be the fin for tomorrow?" 

Anecdotes about Alesh
The new liaison arrived at the secret locale at the corner of the wharf for the first time. The owner of the fin stall laid motionless on a bench with a book on his face. 
The fins being sold had been laid out in a specific arrangement — grayfins faced north; red cupras were paired, and tucked away at the corner was a strangely patterned whitefin. The liaison blinked as he worked his mind to recall the fincode he memorized. Struggle as he could, he failed to read the intel. Just as he was about to call the headquarters, the stall owner tossed him a fin with bits of crushed ice. 
"No great fins today," he said, "Settle your nutritional needs with this one." 
The liaison was stunned for two seconds before he accepted the fin and nodded in gratitude. He walked for around thirty yards before turning around and looked at the stall again — The owner laid on his bench as though the transaction had never taken place. 
Report of the day: Situation normal. Intel is accurate and ... pretty reliable.</>
