6489835183020742264:
<@profile.key>According to Fluorite herself, her Originium Arts solely focused on the creation of polymorphic fluids with adhesive abilities. However, if this were true (which I personally feel to be not), then her malignant social skills could only be explained by her "extensive experiences" — since she has expert-level abilities in identifying damaging information about others. Fluorite's willingness to reveal such information without hesitation is another thing entirely. Even though Arts assimilation test results are publicly accessible, there are those who stubbornly believe that Fluorite has secret "divination" abilities. 
"Just think about it. You almost flopped your PE and the instructor just gave you an earful. While heading back to the dorms you see Fluorite along the corridors and she suddenly goes, 'I guess it can't be helped. Midnight sandwiches are just so divine'... She's so friggin' annoying! Fine, I know I shouldn't be snacking at night but why would she say it out loud?" 
One Operations Team Z7 member once voiced their frustrations, but instead of receiving comfort, they were met with a chorus of knowing looks that silently said, "I feel you." In all fairness, Fluorite does not intend to stir up trouble and she hardly speaks during dialogs. However, her carefree nature combined with her excessively unforgiving tone proved to be rather discomforting to most people during many situations. When these issues were brought before Fluorite, her defense was simple and sincere: 
"You want me to go and apologize?" </>

Too many nosy people around, so I wrote it down on paper. Burn it when you're done reading. 
The Landbreakers took our camp, supplies, and cash. I sneaked over there and saw what they did. The LB scum took apart everything. Even mama's kitchen sink! 
I got a plan to deal with these greedy scab eaters.
We'll leave a few obvious and subtle clues, graffiti, and letters about secret loot stashes that don't exist. Some of the LBs are bound to bite the bait and do their own digging in secret. When that happens, we'll show them what we're made of. 
No one is the eternal prey. And no one is the eternal hunter.

6589571119810097326: We are thankful for the information you have gathered regarding the locals near Yinglung Pass and their views of the YSTF. Prior to your findings, we knew little regarding the public image of the YSTF. Most of our intel on this subject matter comes from external observations or idle chatter from members of the public. In addition to the documents we have found and the information you have kindly shared, we can see that the residents of Wuling, particularly the survivors from the disaster that took place ten years ago, actively volunteered for Yinglung Pass transport missions and often prepared their own supplies for the YSTF. This shows that the Yinglungs have grassroots support, even though they have been strictly prohibited from accepting any donations from the public.

Endministrator! Thanks for the goods you delivered. If we didn't have these materials, we might need to restart a ton of experimental projects. You're a true lifesaver. Truth be told, whenever our experiments run into bottlenecks and threaten the end of our careers, you'd always stand up for us! This timely delivery of cargo, and that data recovery you did for us last time! Can this be predestined? Maybe Fate has decreed that we will carry on our work with your assistance! 
Remember Harper? After he received the goods you delivered, he ran 2 simulations ... and I heard he got some breakthrough. We don't really know the details, though. Harper is just being Harper, ignoring everyone except for his work. I don't know what made him into such workaholic but whatever rocks his little boat! Anyways, thanks a million, Endministrator!

6676613857538089852:
This land might not be anything special. 
Same old plains, same old air, and same old sea. The skies are different, though. There is a huge planet that we could clearly see in the day. There are also no twin moons at night. Life is hardly different from that in Terra. 
Is that a good thing? I don't know. 
I'm here because of national conscription, but It pays and there's food. That's all there's to it. 

But as time went on, I started to feel the differences. 
The land has no Originium. Heck, even the national borders felt like a joke. I thought this was some Columbian frontier pioneering work, where people slogged all day in the wilderness for something to eat. But just today, someone who looked like Leithanian nobility asked me, a worker conscript, about the specifications of machine parts and how to use some machinery. 
And mister nobleman wasn't joking around. 
Ursusian, Leithanian, Columbian... All these divisive labels are now being reduced to crew mate, freund, and pardner. 
I like how things turned out, it's great! But how did it happen? I don't really know. 
I asked around a bit and they're just as confused as I am. So I didn't get my answer for a while until... 

"What is Endfield?" 
I asked the huge Sarkaz team lead this question when I first reported to work. Endfield sounds like a really unlucky name for a company working on the frontiers. 
He did not say anything. Instead, he brought us to the tip of the ship temporarily serving as the Endfield HQ. Told us to have a good look at the planet and the world. 
Planet and world... We hardly used those terms before. 
The team lead saw my confusion and smiled. He told me they are the meaning behind Endfield. 
"Meaning that we gotta use new, pretty words?" 
"Meaning that we left our past on Terra." 

Perhaps he was right. 
Before I decided to walk through the Cosmic Gate to reach this place, I, and everyone else, have forever left something back in Terra. 
People who struggled in the mine shafts, who roamed the wilderness, who worked in the cities, who gave concerts at the spires... Everyone standing shoulder to shoulder aboard the ship, ready to build a future together. 
Fate gave us a choice. We chose something different from the past to create a new way of life. 

What is this world going to be like? don't know. 
Because we're building it right now. 
Buildings will rise from the ground and crops will sprout from the earth. 
This is our resounding answer about the world we're making.

6737210340054032885:
<@profile.key>"How long are you gonna stay this time? You coming home?" 
"...A day. And nah." 
"Just a day? Then never mind ... sigh. Well, a couple of days ago, Rossi was whining again, saying she thinks you'll pull the same stunt this year — you know, 'the gift arrives, but the brother doesn't.' Come on, even if you're not planning to come home, you could at least find time to see her once in a while." 
"It's already been what, over half a year? Since you two last met up at Tommaso's place." 
"I still need to take care of a few things. Once it's done..." 
"Stop right there! That's what you said last time! Then you ran off to the frontier, saying you had to clean up someone's mess..." 
"Gosh, I don't get you young folks. How do you always have so much to do?" 
"Hey, if I were you, no matter how swamped I was, I'd still swing by more to check in on my little sis. She's a kid! Every other kid her age in the clan's doted on by their families all day every day..." 
"You're her family too." 
"Well, yeah — Wait! No, don't try to spin this on me... I get you've got your own things going on. But listen, as someone who's been there — whenever I see little Rossi, I can't help but think of my own little girl... Biggest regret of my life? Not being there for her when I still had the chance." 
"Just hear me out, Catello... Life's short, don't do things you'll end up regretting." 
"I hear you. Uncle Rust."  
"Rossi knows I'm back today." 
"WHAT? No way!" 
"Then why would she run off to the hunting grounds first thing in the morning with the Louis twins? Unless..." 
"Uh-huh. Exactly." 
"Argh... You two really are true Lupus. So stubborn and so damn alike ... it's scary." 
"Alright, alright. Do whatever you like! But if you're always absent, aren't you worried something might go wrong?" 
"I was gonna keep it from you for Rossi's sake ... but looks like you need to hear it. Six months ago, she got herself into serious trouble — stuck her neck out for Old Bart and his men, and ended up on the Shadowskull's hit list. I remember it clear as day. Fourteen of them showed up, each nastier than the last. If Tommaso hadn't pulled some strings and brought in Capone's Fangs, we probably wouldn't have even made it out of the black market alive..." 
"..." 
"Worried now, huh?" 
"Nope. I was just wondering how Rossi found out I was heading north. It was you, wasn't it?" 
"Oh! Um, heh ... well ... yeah, I might've let that slip ... wasn't on purpose though..." 
"Ahem, anyway! I checked your guns! They're as good as new! Just ease up on those custom rounds of yours. No barrel's gonna last with the way you burn through them..." 
"Got it." 
"And ... there were actually fifteen of them." 
"Huh?" 
"Alright, see you around." 
— Audio log from Howlfire Gunsmiths</>

<@intru.bold>- AIC Factory: Auto Management -</>
· When the Endministrator goes offline, the Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) will enter an auto-management mode to keep the AIC Factory running.
· For safety concerns, if the Endministrator is offline for extended periods of time, the PAC will automatically switch off the AIC Factory.
· The Endministrator will automatically resume control over the PAC when going back online. The AIC Factory will resume work normally and the auto-management operation timer will be reset.

· Auto-management duration limit: 7 days

<@intru.bold>- AIC Factory: Regional Restrictions -</>
· Auto-management can only be maintained for AIC Factories in 2 regions due to algorithmic limitations.
· AIC Factory Auto-Management Region 1: The latest unlocked PAC region in the Endministrator's current main mission progress.
· AIC Factory Auto-Management Region 2: Another region where the Endministrator most recently visited.
· All other regional AIC Factories will be temporarily switched off.
· Go to the Protocol Management screen to view the operational status of the AIC Factory in the local region.

DOCUMENT: Records from a Logistics Operator 
Miss Last Rite accidentally damaged several training machines during testing. From the perspective of the Logistics Division, the scope of damage she inflicted is still within reasonable limits. To put it simply, there are other operators who inflict greater losses during routine inspection. Hence, we wonder if you can help inform Miss Last Rite that there is no need for her to keep delivering handwritten letters of apology to us, or actively avoiding members of our division for that matter.

6881272143373970013:
<@profile.key>The Popper Tactic was first proposed by Operator Alesh. Its theoretical basis was based on poppers, a type of lures often used by hobby anglers. According to Alesh's extremely detailed yet unverifiable explanations, the popper is a fake, buoyant fin. Poppers are meant to dance, glimmer, wag their tails, annoy, and finally piss off the foul tempered big fins to the point that they burst out of the deep. Simply put, poppers do this: "Dun wanna move? Whatever. Watch me pollute your turf, play dead, and nab your stuff till you come out and beat me — And then you'll realize that I got a co-star on stage with me." 

What? I need to explain what happened recently? Oh, that was just a basic covert ops job. Convoy needed to get through Route 17 but that LBs love the place. The bandits seized cargo, kidnapped people, and even took over radio stations. Late Night Traffic Radio literally became a stinking cesspit of crude breakkae. 
I was about to go fix my rod, you know, when I got the mission notification. And when I read the words "basic covert operations", I suddenly got overwhelmed by work procrastination. Anyway, before I left for the job, I took a look at the map and mumbled: "Ugh. Let's try the popper." 
Of course, nobody knew what I was talking about. Convoy probably thought it was some new weapon and they agreed without thinking. The naivety is astonishing. I mumble something to myself, and people went along. 
Anyway, it was something really simple: 
We dumped two trucks by the road and dressed them up like they broke down, along with a few crates of "firearms". Eh, don't get too excited. The crates were filled with bricks. We even sprayed "Class I Hazard" on the crates to make it look real, you know. And then we played an "SOS recording" with a trembling voice. Shit is for real, man. More real than that empty rod in my previous angling run. 
And the first breaker mob came as expected. They paced around the setup like a beast lured in by fresh fins, and got all hungry and excited. Everyone of them wanted a piece of the "free takeaway". 
It wasn't even over. I tweaked the SOS radio louder and the second mob arrived. The first group of boys got really pissed. They went from "I wanna bite" to "You're not getting a bite". These blightheads didn't even bother to greet and went straight to all out war at each other. Just like wild, rabid cloudbeasts scrapping for a fin. 
So the crew hid behind the slopes and enjoyed the breaker show. We even commented on their fighting styles while snacking on dried fin roasts — Man, it was buggly as hell. I remembered hustling as a harbor punk when I was a kid, and I still fight better than these dudes. 
When the battle winded down, we swooped in like professional crime scene photo-takers. Take some pictures and clean up the entire place. The dead, the disabled, and the maybe dead or disabled. Anyway, the breaker war didn't leave many who could talk. 
So the entire convoy got through. Some trucker even told me: "Bro, that was some masterful covert ops." 
I said: "Thank you. My OG plan was to look at the skies all day. Didn't expect an entire school of fins to show up." 
The biggest take away from this was me verifying a principle — Everything is like angling. Make things up along the way and finprovise. Way better than making detailed plans, IMO.</>

6887016316197916806:
<@profile.key>Séraphine Xaihi is a member of the Cabal of Tranquility. Since Endfield Industries and the Cabal of Tranquility are jointly carrying out Originium Information Technology research projects, Abbess Vronta recommended Xaihi to work at Endfield premises. Xaihi is currently building the Dijiang Protorig Network and performing various data engineering tasks such as systems integration. 

Xaihi seems to be extremely interested in my mechanical construction and sentience module. During our conversation, she repeatedly inquired how my linguistics converter transformed sentience logic signals into digital code interpretable as audio wave outputs by my voice generator. In every occasion, I politely requested her to focus on the ongoing HR interview. Xaihi appeared rather disappointed and expressed her desire to install a duplicate set of my components into her physical body, her reasons being: "This will prevent me from suddenly voicing bursts of esoteric verbiage, and thus avoid incidents of misunderstanding and disturbance." I pointed out that Xaihi's human organ — namely, her cerebrum, is far more capable of generating expressions more complex and effective than machine-generated language. Miss Xaihi, out of personal etiquette, gave me a smile in response. 
Besides this minor incident, the general profile of Xaihi matches an individual capable of living together with mechanical constructs. This matches her knowledge and background with the Cabal of Tranquility. Her introverted nature, however, differed from other Cabalists whom we have communicated with. The Cabal, after all, is an organization known throughout the world for their openness as well as eagerness to link up with others. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

DOCUMENT: A Study of Qingbo Martial Arts (Excerpt) 
The origins of Qingbo Martial Arts can be traced back to the village known as Qingbo Stockade in the region of Wuling. It is a practiced tradition of the Stockade residents. However, this collection of fighting techniques is so esoteric that it was not recorded in the Addendum to the Martial Codex. 
One must study the history of Qingbo Stockade to understand the unique features of Qingbo Martial Arts. During the lead-up to the Humiliation War and the several years that followed, a group of Yanese refugees who were determined to distance themselves from factional conflicts and warfare traveled to what became the modern region of Wuling. The Wuling Very Large Rift (VLR) did pose a threat but also provided a barrier against hostile raids and attacks. The refugees thus decided to settle and build a village that eventually grew into Qingbo Stockade. According to some, these Stockaders were the original residents of Wuling. 
From this perspective, Qingbo Stockade seems to be a peaceful sanctuary, but this assumption is far from the truth. The Wuling VLR has created a very hostile environment with large numbers of roaming Aggeloi, fearsome wild beasts, and frequent rift activities. The Stockaders have been constantly fighting against these outside "enemies" and these intense battles gradually gave rise to several combat techniques unique to the Qingbo Stockade. Training in these techniques not only strengthens the body but also heightens one's control over their innate Originium Arts. A few of the moves and skills also highlight the unique features of the Qingbo area. 
An example is the famous "Stride of Surging Spring" that allows martial artists to effortlessly stroll across a flowing river while leaving roiling currents in their wake. At first I mistook it for a form of witchcraft that mentally manipulates flowing water. But the Stride turns out to be an Originium Arts technique that focuses Arts energy into the tips of the feet, then channels it into the water with incredible precision to generate resonating vibrations. Another example is the "Jaunt Across Bamboo Leaves" that is literally leapmancy using flimsy bamboo leaves as support. The technique involves applying a "sliver of resilient pulse" of Originium Arts upon fragile-looking bamboo leaves. Such levels of finesse and control, if honed properly, could unleash a terrifying burst of "Pulser Punch" with barely any movement. 
Watchguard Captain Mi Fu of the Wuling Scientific Developmental District happens to be a practitioner of Qingbo Martial Arts. She described the "Pulser Punch" as a "bolt of lightning". When attacking the enemy, the martial artist should treat the body like a "handcannon", soar like a flying Lung, and descend like a lightning blast. This humble author has personally witnessed Captain Mi Fu in action during a mission to eradicate the Aggeloi. She struck them with such force that her moves seem on par with the Tianshi Lei Fa (Thunder Arts). 
In addition to their powerful martial arts, the Stockaders also established a unique tradition of martial competition. For many years, all martial artists of Qingbo Stockade eagerly challenged each other to see who was the best. On the day of the Arena Showdown, every Stockader, regardless of gender or age, would gather to witness the fights and cheer on their favorite contenders. Captain Mi Fu herself was an undisputed champion of the Stockade before she joined Wuling. Unfortunately, the Stockade arena was undergoing major repairs due to recent damage when the author was penning this section. Deprived of the opportunity to visit the site in person, I could only ask Captain Mi Fu to send me photographs or other image data of the repaired arena. 
I once expressed to Captain Mi Fu my wish to interview the Grand Master of Qingbo Martial Arts, the "River-Tamer" himself. However, the Captain told me that the Grand Master became a hermit and even she did not know where he went. The Captain also showed me a postcard sent by her Senior Master that talked about their travels. The Grand Master's attention was held, for more than an hour, by two kids fighting each other at some alleyway of the Civilization Band. Before leaving, the old man sighed and said: "Trained all my life, but still I envy these kids. They fight when they want and remain friends afterwards. A beautiful sight indeed." 
Finally, I gave Captain Mi Fu a proposal for organizing and compiling information related to Qingbo Martial Arts, and including it within the Continuation for the Addendum to the Martial Codex. The Captain happily agreed and expressed that it was her wish to have more people to protect Wuling with their fists. When that day arrives, she could leave the place (Wuling) and restart her lifelong wish of fighting whenever she wanted.

PATIENT NAME: Ruy van Catcher 
AGE: 4 yo 
ADMISSION DATE: Aug 15 
INSTITUTION: Browning Hospital, Free City of Alloycarrot 
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: Raman Harrent 

PRESENT ILLNESS: Patient was injured by an accidental Originium device failure 3 hours ago, resulting in minor injuries to the cervical vertebrae. Patient symptoms 1 hour after admission include: dizziness, hyperhidrosis, weakness of the lower limbs, and nausea. Patient also exhibits giddiness and reduced consciousness after onset of symptoms. Tests reveal continuous reduction in muscle strength. Recommend immediate hospitalization and surgical treatment. 

DIAGNOSIS: Acute Oripathy with accompanying complications. Continue monitoring. 

DOCUMENT: Medical logs from 10 years ago 
"Young Master Catcher, as your attending physician, I believe I am obliged to give you these reminders. Conclusions based on our current medical monitoring tell us that your Oripathy is now under control. However, your physical strength and fortitude are now far below average. You might need to spend quite a bit of time in bed. I'll also advise against getting off bed or moving around. Any action that unnecessarily stresses your body might endanger your life... I hope you can pay attention to that, you and your family." 

DOCUMENT: Medical logs from 6 years ago 
"Once again I must emphasize, Young Master... Okay. Perhaps you prefer it that I address you as Mr. Catcher. Well then, Mr. Catcher, though there is no sign that your sickness has worsened, your physical exercises have far exceeded the levels I've recommended for your case. It is with my strongest advice that you attend at least 3 rounds of full physical examinations and my personal inquiries at the hospital in the coming months. And ... if you decide to persist in rehabilitative exercises of such excessive nature, please make sure you do so in the presence of a family member or professional caretaker within ten yards of yourself. I am also prescribing you another list of medication. But also keep in mind that given the state of your health, even the most effective pharmaceutical products would have limited use... 

DOCUMENT: Medical logs from 2 years ago 
"Okay. I am now forced to admit there are certain things ... certain accidents in the world that go against medical norms. Mr. Catcher, it is by 'miracle' ... but also primarily your personal force of will and determination, that you have molded your body to its current standards... I did not expect to see a second case of such phenomenal and dramatic recovery as yours. Of course, as your attending physician, I still hope you'll come in for your semiannual examinations, just to be on the safe side." </>

· Each purchase of the Monthly Pass immediately gives [Origeometry]×12 and Monthly Pass perks for 30 days.
· While Monthly Pass perks are active, log in every day to claim [Oroberyl]×200, [Emergency Sanity Booster]×1, and [Sanity Sanity Usage Permit]×4.
· Monthly Pass daily login rewards are refreshed daily at 4:00 AM (Server Time).

<@intru.bold>- Special Reminders -</>
· While Monthly Pass perks are active, rewards missed for not logging in will not be returned.
· Monthly Pass perks can only last for a total of 180 days. Another Monthly Pass can only be purchased when the valid period of the Monthly Pass perks ≤150 days.
· For excessive purchases due to special circumstances, the valid period of the Monthly Pass perks will not stack. Instead, an amount of [Origeometry] based on the value of the purchase will be returned to you.

7199621734345414955:
The cleric named "Sessionite" thus entered the wildlands.
And he arrived at an abandoned village. The creek had long dried up and the soil was cracked.
And the Fortes, suffering hunger and thirst, asked him for salvation.
"You need knowledge that will lead you to where the water flows and the thick grasses grow."
"I shall teach you how to read these lands."
And so his hand gleamed with the light of the Originium Network,
And he imparted upon them knowledge that came from those from afar.
After seven days and seven nights,
The enlightened Fortes learned how to dig a water well in the barrens,
And thus the water flowed and the grasses grew forever more.

The cleric named "Resurgonite" thus entered the wildlands.
And he arrived at an abandoned village. The creek had long dried up and the soil was cracked.
And the Fortes, suffering hunger and thirst, asked him for salvation.
"You need strength to retake the watering hole that you once called yours."
"I shall grant you weapons to protect your kin."
And then he removed the guns he carried on his back,
And together with the relics of the old, he gave them to the Fortes he met.
After seven days and seven nights,
The armed Fortes drove the Landbreakers that occupied the upper flows of the river and chased them out of these lands,
And thus the water flowed and the grasses grew forever more.

The cleric named "Veritarite" thus entered the wildlands.
And he arrived at an abandoned village. The creek had long dried up and the soil was cracked.
And the Fortes, suffering hunger and thirst, asked him for salvation.
"You must converge your faith as one, and pray for a rain of abundance."
"I shall be with you, and lead you to wait for the destined season of rain."
And then he codified the Law and the commandments, and taught them unity and forbearance,
And he patiently encouraged every Forte whose despair was great in their hearts, and gave them peace and hope.
After seven days and seven nights,
A torrent of life-giving rain fell for the persevering Fortes,
And thus the water flowed and the grasses grew forever more.

If you are the thirsty Forte,
Which of these clerics' teachings will you accept?

— The Parable of the Three Clerics, engraved at one corner of the Sanctilamina Cathedralis Tranquillae

<@profile.key>Operator Snowshine works for Rhodes Island. As a SAR professional, she has engaged in multiple search and rescue (SAR) missions jointly carried out by Rhodes Island and Endfield Industries, resolving a range of complex issues in the process. Snowshine now serves as Endfield Industries' SAR liaison. 

Operator Snowshine has, in multiple occasions, demonstrated her extensive experiences in conducting SAR operations in the north. She has proved the value of her survival skills and her ability to quickly respond to various contingencies. 
Snowshine enthusiastically recounted her successful SAR mission rescuing the Oathkeepers and polar caravans, highlighting her careful planning, precise execution, and strategic decisions in overcoming natural hazards. Snowshine's engineering background gives her vital technical expertise that has been key in her SAR missions. The operator is extremely passionate and dedicated to her work. Her deep experience in both engineering and SAR operations makes her a valuable asset to Endfield. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· Complete the main mission [Chapter Ⅱ Process Ⅱ: Where Danger Sleeps].</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, complete various missions, produce support goods, and trade them with the outpost for generous rewards.</>
<@gray.note>· The event is divided into 3 phases. Completing the Phase 1 mission gives rewards as well as a limited-time formula for special support goods. Use the AIC Factory to produce and trade the said goods with the outpost to earn [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs], the latter can be spent at the Goods Exchange for useful goods.</>
<@gray.note>· On day 8 of the event, the Goods Exchange will have new exchangeable goods.</>
<@gray.note>· Completing the Phase 1 mission also starts the Phase 2 mission. Completing the Phase 2 mission gives rewards and an advanced limited-time formula for an advanced type of special support goods. Produce and trade the said goods with the outpost for more [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs], the latter can be spent at the Goods Exchange for useful goods.</>
<@gray.note>· After reaching certain Fruits of Altruism Certs criteria, the mission of the last Phase will begin. Complete this mission to earn rewards.</>
<@gray.note>· The event ends on May 13 at 16:00 (server time). Any support goods already produced but not yet traded to the outpost will be auto-converted to [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs] and sent to the in-game Mail. If the amount of [Wuling Stock Bills] from this conversion exceeds the stock bill limit, the excess portion will be auto-converted to [Fruits of Altruism Certs].</>
<@gray.note>· After the event ends, the Goods Exchange will remain open for a while so you can trade for the remaining supplies. Please complete all your exchanges before May 19 at 04:00 (server time).</>

<@profile.key>After joining Endfield, it only took a few months for Miss Snowshine to give many operators a friendly and warm impression. 
Snowshine often helps others in her spare time, usually with simple errands and deliveries. Though they may seem small, these tasks often prove to be real lifesavers, making people truly grateful to have Snowshine around. 
"...That bottle of vinegar Miss Snowshine delivered was just in time. My sweet and sour meat would have been ruined without it!" reported the canteen chef. 
Miss Snowshine also spared no effort trying to popularize SAR knowledge and polar survival skills, and often gave everyone proper demonstrations — these include crucial first-aid procedures as well as quick turn techniques for snowboarding. 
"BY TALOS...! How did she do that? The last time I saw such a beautiful turn was ... during the finals of the Band Snowboard Championships! She's a guaranteed medalist if she ever attends, I'll bet my money on this!" said an anonymous operator after watching Snowshine's demonstration. 
Miss Yvonne from the Specialist Tech Division also has plenty of good things to say about Snowshine: 
"Oh ho! This is the first time I've met someone capable of using ice-making Arts Units with such expertise! Alrighty! I hope Snowshine can test more of my ice makers! She'll shave at least ten days off my testing phase!" 
However, canteen staff responsible for stocking the shelves would post warnings when Snowshine returns to Dijiang. Extra attention must be given to prevent the "dessert bar from being left with only the labels". </>

7371108661583598285:
<@profile.key>An Investigation on the Joint Applications and Efficiency of Xiranite Machine Automation Control Systems in the Automated Industry Complex 
ABSTRACT: This investigation specifically focuses on the automation of Xiranite machinery and discusses potential possibilities and concepts for jointly applying said machinery in the Automated Industry Complex. This paper explores actual scenarios and applications for inter-disciplinary and multi-sector efforts for integrated Blight containment and remediation, and summarizes methodologies that improves safety guarantees as well as the efficiency of coordinated operations. The aim... 
Keywords: mechanical engineering; Endfield Industries; rift; Blight; cooking; food safety; efficiency optimization; 
............ 
REFERENCES: 
[1] Endfield Industries. Ten Year Plan and Recommended Milestones for the Automated Industry Complex. 
[2] Endfield Industries. Sharing of Open Source Technologies of AIC Automation Approaches. 
[3] Endfield Industries. An Overview of Applied Technologies of Originium Circuitry. 
[4] Huang, M.Z. and Luo, S.Q., Field Applications of Xiranite Protoforms in the Remediation of Blight Trauma. 
............ 
ACKNOWLEDGMENT: It isn't an everyday thing when you can simply open the dorm room to converse with authors of the works you cited in your younger days. I know I helped solve a few problems here and there, but looking at the bigger picture, I actually received way more assistance in return... 

"The paper... Argh. Don't flatter me, Endmin. I'll get really embarrassed. It's just based on the basic theories you came up with years ago. This is far from a breakthrough." 
"..." 
"It's kinda funny, really. I'm already forty plus, yet I'm still obsessed about climbing the highest mountain. There's a little panda deep within me... If I ever mentioned this to people in HAS, I would end up as joke material. They'd laugh and say how far Pan Yuan had fallen." 
"..." 
"Mountains would only get higher, so the highest peak is always beyond reach? Ohoho. I admire your confidence! But the more you say it, the greater my urge to challenge the hike. By the way, I've been looking up about your Automated Industry Complex and worked out a brand new tech. It's something you never wrote about..." 
"..." 
"I'm definitely not your match. I call this joint growth through competition! If you want me to be honest with you, it's because ... your AIC Factory is terrific. I like this tech. From the bottom of my heart. If only we had this tech back then... If I could get this AIC when I was twenty..." 
"..." 
"You're right. It's never too late...!" </>

7376938487214163593:
The mountain of a man laid against the wreck of the trailer truck. Blood oozed from the gaps of his heavy armor.
The man did not move. His unwavering gaze stared at the north.
"You never expected to be defeated, right?" Jerreth coughed and spat. He wiped the bloody bubbles of spit from his lips with a muddied sleeve and approached the huge figure. The shattered remains of dead Aggeloi surrounded him.
Jerreth picked up the man's broken weapon and tossed it aside to make room for a seat.
"We were heading south. WE WERE HEADING TOWARDS THE BLASTED SOUTH! Nobody knew we were going south until we started down the road!"
Jerreth toyed with the pen in his hand. Without ink, it was little more than an empty tube.
"I ... I don't know what to write... How should I report this? Gah... I should get the hell out of this place."
The supplies on the trailer truck were completely destroyed. One of the ropes for securing the cargo crates dangled next to Jerreth. 
"You should have been the one who survived... You're the hero of the army... You were the only one who could see this through... the only one..."
"I'm just a journalist! How would I write about this crushing defeat? They'll murder my career for robbing the glory of this magnificent battle!"
Jerreth buried his face in his hands and howled in agony. He did not stop shaking since the fight began.
After quite a while, he finally stood up and felt the northern breeze brushing against him.
"There was this story. The hero's name was finally recited in an epic paean, his deeds glorified and enshrined by the lines. There is no such thing as a silent and ignominious death."

"I'm going home."
He mumbled to himself as he cast everything aside. He threw away his memories, career, and responsibilities at this very site right next to the suit of heavy armor.
"I'm going home..."
And thus he left a trail of footprints as he trudged towards a certain direction. He was indeed going home.

7409860022984909046:
<@profile.key>It was another busy day as usual aboard Dijiang. Not many people took notice of the new operator. Lifeng also did not intend to do anything spectacular. But he soon caught some attention by the third day he came aboard the ship — People started talking about a lad doing morning exercises in the Growth Chamber. On arrival, the team found an Anasa meditating cross-legged in the chamber, eyes shut, breath steady. Those who were there reported seeing a soft, gentle glow emanating from his body. No one knew if they were seeing things or if the phenomenon was a result of artificial lighting from the cultivation tanks... 
"He's Sarkaz, that's why. Nothing to be worried about. It must be his 'Spirit'!" 
"It might be some secret Yanese technique. I think it's called the 'Ki'..."
"It doesn't matter what it's called. The moment he sat down ... it brought about a vibe of serenity... It's like a form of artsy power that made me wanna call him 'Young Master'..." 
"Young Master? I think he's more like the Young Warrior from the martial arts movies that Chen showed us yesterday." 
"Shh! I think he heard us! And he's turning around! QUICK! HIDE..."
After a brief, noisy moment, the lance made a few random slashes on the lighting module. Three fluid inlets and outlets of the control module were split open. They saw the youth fumbling to get back to his feet to wave off the white, billowing smoke around him. The youth then stood at attention: "I'm so sorry! I thought nobody was using this place!" 
They realized he was just a boy, but their realizations quickly gave way to confusion: "How do you look so young and old at the same time?!" 
Lifeng looked confused. "Because ... the Anasas are an old and wise race, but I'm still a little young...?" 
It was an enlightening answer — Meaningful in some way, but also wholly incapable of answering the question! 

Lifeng the Young Master (or Young Warrior) gave off an aura of rarely observed contradiction. He was ancient yet young, stiff yet flexible. These quirks were further verified as people started to befriend him. 
The first to notice Lifeng's ability was provisional combat instructor Lil' Dodge. The instructor gave nothing but praise about Operator Lifeng's spear fighting skills after a friendly spar. The instructor (notorious for strictness) lavished extremely rare praises in Operator Lifeng's multi-dimensional assessment report. Things like: "Lance strikes way faster than the average"; "Hits with just the right force"; "A warrior of rare quality"; and — "I really wanna know where he learned how to fight!" 
Lifeng was extremely surprised by these commendations, and said that he was but a little boy playing with sticks when compared to his master, Da Pan. 
After becoming the target of endless questioning (and all sorts of bribes), Da Pan finally agreed to reveal certain secrets. The Ursus confessed he was not good with children but Zhuang Fangyi gave him the important task of training Lifeng. Finding himself stuck with an energetic and inquisitive kid who followed him everywhere, Da Pan found a solution. He purchased over 600 video tapes of folk festivals and plays from a Hongshan antique store and allowed Lifeng to watch TV all day. He did not expect the kid to become engrossed with what he saw and took it all in. One day, Lifeng grabbed a cloth drying pole to fool around with. It was at that moment when Da Pan realized the boy's potential. 
"Let's just keep it down," Da Pan waved his meaty paw around, "The kid barely scratched the surface of the martial arts. Sure, his attacks look great and the flourishes are pretty, but true combat got nothing to do with that." 
"So what is true combat all about?" 
"It's all about 'knowing'. True combat is about defeating the opponent, not the spear. A spear wielder studies the spear manuals. A true fighter studies the foe." 
"That's really deep, Master Da Pan. How exactly do we study that?" 
"Well, it boils down to personality. Just you watch. He's just a kid, but Lifeng has the patience for it. And he's a meticulous one, too."</>

"Huff... huff... Jennings. About time you picked up the phone.
"Listen. This is a corporate war. No... it's ... it's a political witch hunt! They learned about our discovery! They know how this would change the world!
"Those blighted buggers got their eyes on our research project! They're looking for us! Looking all over the world for us!
"I'll make these buggers regret their choices! I won't forget those blasted faces. I spent years working on the thing. NO! NOT RAYTHEAN! They won't protect us. We're dead meat once we hand our tech over!
"NO! I won't let them have it! NEVER! Look, I'm heading over to your place right now. Nutty got us a car. We must leave the Band. I got friends outside who'll pick us up.
"Who? Those warmongers, that's who. I know you don't like them, but at least the muscleheads are more reliable than the corpo-scum pulling the strings from their glassy penthouses!
"Choice? THERE IS NO OTHER CHOICE! Our tech is going to upset their entire setup! They want to keep their monopoly and they'll regret their decisions. But WE MUST STAY ALIVE, Jennings. We gotta keep ourselves breathing to enjoy the future!
"Jennings...? Hey? You there? Can you hear me? Jennings?"
"BWOOP... BWOOP... BWOOP..."
The man saw his own reflection on the windowpane. He had not slept for days and his eyes resembled lifeless orbs sinking in a dark mire. For a split second, he failed to recognize the ghastly image of himself.
"Blasted blightery!"

7478848393151204136:
Your graduation exams take place tomorrow, my dear. The importance of this review can't be overemphasized as the results will determine the job you'll be assigned with. I hoped to drive you to your exam but my Blight Tide monitoring job got in the way.
I believe you've already made all the essential preparations, but I must highlight the difficult challenge posed by the Originium Arts precision assessment. This is where most candidates fail, but you must succeed if you wish to join the Yinglung Special Task Force.
For this assessment, you'll be using an Arts Unit that the Hongshan Swordmancers specifically developed for Wuling. This unit includes Xiranite components whose circuitries change with the material. Wielding it would put your focus and all your senses to the test.
You probably know all of this by now. Youths of your age are calling this Arts Unit as Monaihe and I can't help but agree. You won't find another Arts Unit capable of eliciting more helpless frustration and anger from its wielder. The nickname even caught on with grown-ups like us.
I'm afraid I can't tell you more about this puzzle-box of an Arts Unit just to keep the assessment fair. Nevertheless, I have complete faith in you. Remember the wooden lock puzzles I bought you when you were a child? They kept you busy for a while but you always found a way to take them apart. Monaihe is the same thing. Overcome it like how you overcame those lock puzzles. Focus on the challenge and enjoy the process.
Good luck out there.

7483721573468990055: I believe you already have the basic understanding of the Very Large Rift (VLR). The huge formation of multiple rifts measures nearly one hundred kilometers in length and exists right below Wuling. In other words, the city is literally built on an "active volcano" — To be more accurate, the entire place is built right over a "volcanic crater". So, why would such a precarious city develop such a bustling shopping avenue? Why is everyone settling in with a peace of mind...? We checked past records about Wuling and arrive at these verified findings — The Viceroy Tianshi Zhuang Fangyi has exceptional administrative skills. Ever since the founding of Wuling, she wholly focused on providing living amenities, education, and business opportunities. Her comprehensive ideas and approach are most admirable. Of course, I am not downplaying the contributions of the Wulingers... According to verifiable data we have, Tianshi Zhuang always came up with the most applicable development plans for special focuses that require them most. These efforts include the adding new bamboo raft stops, or introducing new Chubby Lung designs for Fangxing Avenue... I believe every Wulinger (though this claim appears exaggerated, it is not) has placed their trust in her for the city to reach its present day level of development. You yourself read the memos of the traveling merchant or the proprietor... Everyone inside and outside the city believes that the shopping avenue is heading up. A commercial wonder in a precipitous place. I believe this is the impression that people get when they first visit Fangxing Avenue.

The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: This plate of fried rice proved exemplary among the other dishes submitted in this cook-off! The stir-fried grains of beautiful amber rice glistened with a golden hue, while powdered yazhen enhanced both the color and aroma of this culinary work.
We must admit that everyday dishes truly put a chef's skills to the test. A fine plate of garden fried rice should be a ballroom of amber grains dancing on the tongue — but our contending chef probably went for a mass-production approach, and the result lacked that kiss of true Wok Hey.

7728293751864192684:
<@intru.bold>- Stockpile Transfer -</>
· Stockpile Transfer is a method of transferring items between depots. It moves selected items from the source depot to the destination depot.

<@intru.bold>- Metastorage Transfer -</>
· Raising the Regional Development Level of the source region unlocks a new transfer mode: Metastorage Transfer.
· Metastorage Transfer can transfer a selected quantity of a selected type of item that can be produced at the source region, and move them to the destination depot.
· Metastorage Transfer is completed without reducing the stockpile of the selected item in the source depot, and can even be completed even if the source depot has none of the selected item.

Endministrator: 
Hope this letter finds you well.
Test Area research has stalled for quite a while. Progress would have been out of reach without your timely assistance. With your intervention, many projects that were once put on hold due to equipment and facilities failures can proceed once more. I believe it won't take long before our research achieves a breakthrough. And once that happens, our people at Wuling will no longer need to worry about a recurrence of the recent incident.
Once again, thank you for your help.
Sincerely
Best Regards

7833282538905230497: The frontiersmates also chose to learn from the mistakes of the past and look for new solutions. The development of the Controlled Originium Distribution (CORD) technology gave a degree of control over the growth of Originium clusters and made it possible to "cultivate" them in specific areas. CORD greatly reduced the likelihood of Catastrophes in Talos-II. Additionally, Long-term Oripathy Treatment (LORIT) provided by Rhodes Island has been well-received by the Talosians. Preventive Oripathy Shots (PORIPS) and therapeutic suppressants were also developed. The Infected were no longer stigmatized in Talosian towns and villages now that treatment became readily available. Though the situation looks hopeful, Originium-related issues and challenges continued to affect the new world.

7902391980426235494:
To the Respectable Tianshi of Hongshan Academy of Sciences: 
Salutations. 
Though I have pondered long and hard, I do not know to whom this letter should be addressed to. In the end, I asked an acquaintance from your esteemed institution to pen this letter and to attach herein a silk banner (which I have ordered) along with a minor token of my thanks. With great sincerity I hope my gratefulness can be conveyed to the three heroes who saved my life. 
I am a traveling merchant of the TGCC who has spent many years traveling from city to city. Not too long ago, I stopped by a mining town near Alloycarrot to rest and resupply. I did not expect to be ambushed by the Aggeloi that same night. The sudden worsening of the weather only delayed the arrival of a TGCC corporate security detail. The townsfolk and I were forced to hide and protect ourselves. 
I hid in an elevated position inside an abandoned factory building on the edge of the town. The hiding place offered me great vantage to witness everything that happened afterwards. 
From the mass of Blight that suddenly appeared outside the town, three individuals emerged and sauntered into the town. They wore thick, heavy equipment that I had never seen before, yet moved with remarkable speed and agility. I saw faint flickers of light in the darkness accompanied by deep, reverberating roars that reminded me of manglerbeasts. I'm afraid I cannot accurately describe what that was. I only know they wielded extraordinary weapons. The trio then broke formation. One of them charged right into the Aggeloid host, while the other two took the high ground, striking down any Aggelos that accidentally loitered too close to the people. They moved with incredible coordination and tacit understanding, as though they had done the same thing countless times. During my many years as a traveling merchant, I've seen how the Worker's Guard or corporate security fight on multiple occasions. But what I saw that night, especially that individual who went straight to the place where the Aggeloi was the thickest, elevated battle to a whole different level. I can still recall the moves and strikes vividly. It was just like the old Yanese saying. The warrior fought like an "army of one". 
The trio quickly quelled the Aggeloid unrest but we kept hiding until daybreak. A few courageous townsfolk had to sneak out and look around before the rest of us dared to emerge from our hiding places. The threat was eliminated, reduced to rocks and gravel scattered throughout the place. The trio of warriors, however, were gone as well. Although I do not know their name, I am certain they were not fellow townsfolk nor Sarkazian mercenaries as claimed by the townsfolk. From the way they conducted themselves and the equipment they wore, I am certain they hailed from HAS. 
They saved me and everyone living in that mining town. This is an act of charity that we cannot afford to forget. I continued my travels and asked around along the caravan road, but nobody has heard about them. After much deliberation, I am reduced to one final resort. This is why I have sent the letter of appreciation and the silk banner to HAS. I pray the Tianshi can convey our gratitude to the trio who tread upon the Blight. This letter is curt, the credits are meager, and the banner might not look much, but this is the best we can do. 
If fortune smiles on us and this letter is successfully delivered to the intended recipient, please tell them: "We saw what you did that night, and we shall always remember." 
Respectfully Yours. 
A Traveling Merchant. 

"...WHA?! Why did you give this to me? I was just walking by and taught them a few tricks. It's the three of them? No wonder they felt so happy writing up the report. Fine, whatever. I'm busy enough as it is. Handle it your way and stop bothering me! If people hear about this, they'll think I got plenty of time on my hands!"

7903732833042427503: Fortunately, you have also acquired new findings. Through our analysis, we can say with certainty that the building of Qingbo Stockade went through at least two important phases. The first phase involved the elimination of nearby threats, which were likely to be packs of beasts that roamed the area... However, this does not mean that the Blight or Aggeloi posed less of a risk. The local fauna of Qingbo Stockade may have once endangered the livelihood of the local residents to a degree that goes beyond our imagination. The rakerbeasts, howlerbeasts, and waterlamps you have encountered strongly suggest this possibility. Meanwhile, evidence has been found in the database about an Aldertone of the Hannabit Circuit who visited Wuling over 80 years ago. The hanna folksong he left behind includes this line: "An ocean of beasts, a source of the creeks." We were uncertain what the hanna meant until you uploaded your findings. The song is now solid proof how Qingbo Stockade was once threatened by beasts. We postulate that beast taming techniques were developed by Stockaders who tried to fight against these bestial hazards.

7903980368383618597:
<@profile.key>Antal is not as cold or unsociable as he appears. Everyone who actually talked to him would agree with this statement. No one expected this stern-faced dude obsessed with Originium technology and sciences to be equally passionate about lame jokes! Why would this techie tell jokes so bad that one would need to devote all their brain juices and logical reasoning to fathom? Is he doing this to boost his sense of humor? When you look at his expression-less face and realize that he's doing his best to make you laugh, you might also find it difficult to determine why you end up laughing anyway. Is it because of his lame jokes? Or the stark contrast presented by his physical form? 
Akekuri, Antal's team captain, has maintained extreme optimism about this quirk: "At least he's sincere. Though there are times when he fails to read the context. For example, he may get a sudden urge to show off his sense of humor in the face of hostility. Unfortunately, the enemies often think otherwise." 
To Antal, "being someone interesting" may have become a lifelong pursuit, and he treats everyone as instructors capable of reviewing his performance. It doesn't matter if they are fellow teammates of Op Team Z7 or fellow Endfielders whom he only met once — Antal would humbly seek advice from anyone deemed capable of sharing valuable recommendations. 
Recently, Antal placed his antics on hold as a particular individual stepped up to the game and volunteered to offer constant feedback... Hold on. It's voluntary, right? I don't know if there are other people capable of laughing even before Antal finishes his bad jokes. Yes, we're talking about our Endministrator, who would, with the greatest patience, hang out with Antal, and listen to everything he has to say — ranging from eureka moments of inventive ideas to hour-long science lectures that he suddenly initiates when he finds an opportunity to do so. In a particular memorable moment, Antal tried to persuade the Endmin to take off the mask. His excuse was to apply certain mods like a pair of beam-firing eyes... Of course, Akekuri happened to be nearby and she fiercely reprimanded Antal's action. The Operator was forced to write a 6000-word self-examination essay and read it aloud before the Endmin on the following day. 
Of course, this punishment was hardly effective to Antal. It would only take a few days before his creative mind starts to formulate other interesting ideas. </>

7939246450257217692:
<@profile.key>Letter from TGCC 
SENDER: Shiba Trading 
RECIPIENT: Human Resources Division at Endfield Industries 

Dear HR Team at Endfield Industries, 
Greetings. 
My name is Shiba Yoko, mother of Shiba Momiji. Pardon me for reaching out in this manner. 
As the CEO of Shiba Trading, I am accustomed to addressing matters with logic and efficiency. Yet as I write this letter, I find myself overwhelmed by emotions. 
Momiji is my only daughter. Her father met my younger brother while serving in the war against the Aggeloi. That bond eventually brought him into my life, and later, we had Momiji. While I was the strict teacher in her upbringing, her father and uncle were her playmates and protectors, filling her childhood with laughter and idealism. 
Everything changed after the Crusade against the Aggeloi ended ten years ago. Her uncle never returned, and her father came back a different man — only to leave home not long after. 
Since then, I have raised Momiji with care and intention, preparing her to carry forward both our family and our business. I shielded her from the very things that once tore our lives apart. She set aside her childhood game of "fighting Aggeloi" and turned her focus to her studies, growing into a diligent, outstanding young woman. She has never once let me down. 
And yet, upon graduation, she made an unexpected decision — joining Endfield Industries and volunteering for front-line duty. 
I was taken aback and deeply upset. We argued incessantly, and our relationship suffered. 
But over time, I came to realize this was not just a reckless impulse. The same sense of justice and idealism that once guided her father lives on in her. Though these ideals may have cost her a carefree childhood, they now give her purpose — and strength. 
I no longer oppose her decision. She is determined to prove her worth, even if it means risking everything. That is a resolve I cannot deny. 
I respectfully ask that you keep her safe, as much as circumstances allow. But please understand that my daughter is more than just academically gifted. 
Thank you for supporting her journey. 

 Shiba Yoko</>

<@intru.bold>- Stage Rating -</> 
<@gray.note>· Every stage has 3 difficulty tiers: Normal (rewards 1★ when completed), Hard (rewards 2★ when completed), and Brutal (rewards 3★ when completed).</> 
<@gray.note>· Completing a stage in Brutal difficulty and fulfilling the bonus challenge goals grants the [Lustrous] boost to the rating.</> 
<@gray.note>· The highest Total Rating that can be earned from a cycle (by successfully completing 3 stages in Brutal difficulty) is 9★.</> 
<@gray.note>· After a cycle ends, the Total Rating will be reset for the new cycle. Challenge the stages of the new cycle to earn stars again. Total Rating from past cycles will be recorded for determining the [Final Rating] of the current season.</> 

<@intru.bold>- Unlocking Stages -</> 
<@gray.note>· When a cycle begins, Normal and Hard difficulty tiers will be available by default for the stages. Complete a stage in Hard difficulty to unlock its Brutal difficulty.</> 
<@gray.note>· Earning the [Lustrous] title for the current season automatically unlocks Brutal difficulty for all stages in the next season.</>

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· After the Event Center is unlocked</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, you can share your exclusive Invite Code to invite [Returning Members] to the event, and earn rewards that include Oroberyls.</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, [Returning Members] can enter and link another player's Invite Code for an exclusive reward of Oroberyls. Once linked, the code cannot be changed.</>
<@gray.note>· Rewards will be sent to the in-game mail of the corresponding account within 10 minutes. The reward mail is valid for 30 days after exchanging the said event rewards.</>
<@gray.note>· The [Returning Member] status and mission completion status within the event may take time to update. Endministrators can try waiting 10 minutes, then refresh and check again.</>

<@intru.bold>- [Returning Member] Details -</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrators who are eligible for the [Protocol Reconnection] event will be considered [Returning Members]. The participation requirements for [Protocol Reconnection] are as follows:</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrator Authority Level ≥ 3</>
<@gray.note>· Complete main mission [Chapter Ⅰ Process Ⅰ: Valley Reboot]</>
<@gray.note>· Not logged into the game for ≥ 21 consecutive days</>
<@gray.note>· Participation in the last [Protocol Reconnection] event ≥ 42 days ago</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, if a player activates the [Returning Member] status multiple times, their event data will carry over from the previous status.</>

8162620085059109328:
<@profile.key>Xaihi is a calm and reliable operator. She treats her profession with the most zealous of faith, and handles her personal beliefs in the most professional manner. Four years ago, Xaihi came to Endfield Industries as a visiting scholar to support Originium technology projects that we jointly pursue with the Cabal of Tranquility. We did not give her any actual responsibilities back then. However, she immediately committed herself to the repairs of Dijiang mere moments after her arrival. I am inclined to believe that Cabalists have an innate sense of duty, given their knowledge of our past and their hopes for our future. 
— Perlica 

Her way of thinking is completely different from mine. However, we got along fine. It's nothing complicated. Sometimes she pointed out details that I never saw. Hmm. An example? Let me think. I got one. She said: "People reside in their respective languages." That line alone got me thinking for an entire day. 
— Arclight 

Wha? Did she really say that? Now I'm curious! Arclight and Xaihi, our two mega opposite techno-gals, can actually talk to each other! HOW?! Well, I guess Operator Xaihi does talk to everyone. That's what makes her so awesome... And convos with Xaihi don't sound as though she's just blabbering on about her own stuff... I guess people do get some tranquil vibes from her. 
— Chen Qianyu 

I often converse with Operator Xaihi during work. We are both interested in Originium devices. No, that doesn't make us colleagues. Her knowledge and expertise in servers and electronic systems far surpass mine. What do you mean it's rare for me to say something positive about other people? Hurhurhur. I regard myself as an easygoing and casual individual. 
...Since you started this topic, I'm actually curious why she chose to bunk in the server room. Yes, I'm talking about the place where all the server professionals worked at. Look, if I apply for an office equipment for specialized electrical work processes, instead of telling me to pick up the thing at the Logistics Division, I have to pick up the device from her place. That place is devoid of natural solar radiation and has non-stop air conditioning to keep it cold. I have not entered there. Exposing a Savra to such conditions for extended periods of time will induce severe lethargy. I must say that living in such environments is a strange and unique choice. Perhaps she is trying to maintain a long-lost tradition of asceticism. 
Hey, don't leave. I'm not done yet. Operator Xaihi also has a good tastes in gaming. She is one of the very few skillful adversaries I can respect aboard Dijiang. What game, you say? Sudoku. There is no other real game but Sudoku. 
— Antal 

Her hands are cold. She said redemption is cold. I shall live with this fact. 
— Ember</>

Though the floating city has retained its functions as a massive military airship, it is also a window. The city contains a theme park, a theater, and a bazaar open to the entire Civilization Band. 8347531202724930390:
The Sarkaz was a mountain. He picked me up with one hand and stomped towards a less conspicuous place in Seš'qa.
The children nearby pointed at us excitedly. They jabbered to their parents about what they saw and probably mistook this altercation as an audience moment featured in the "Parade of the Royal Courts". I was the "lucky" guy who got to interact with the Kings of the Royal Court.
I was also just as excited when my old man brought me to this flying theme park for the first time.
But now, it felt as though I was about to be executed by this emotionless and silent Sarkaz warrior.
"SORRY! I should've bought a ticket!"
"..."
I didn't know if I would end up like the victim shown in the oldies. I could not remember its title, but the poor lad was completely drained of all his blood by an elegant Vampire to fill his dinner goblet.
"I ... I had fun at this place when I was a kid! 'Errant's Grand Adventure' was my favorite attraction! I just wanna relive my childhood again!"
"..."
I wondered if I would end up like the guy shown in super popular "Records of the Kazdelian Wars" manga. The dude was chopped to pieces by a sword as long as the dude himself, thrown into a furnace for fuel, and burned to raging hot ash that scattered to the winds!
"NO! PLEASE! I CONFESS! I pick-pocketed parents distracted by screaming kids when they crowded up at the ticket booths! I'M SORRY!"
"..."
That did not work. Perhaps my real intent was already foretold by a Cyclops who secretly dwelt in this City of Witchcraft. That would be the end of me! To hold on to dear life, I decided to spill everything!
"STAAAAHHHP! I'LL TELL YOU THE TRUTH! I'm completely broke! And I'm a ... a damn criminal! I might have schemed to steal a creator-signed 'Sundered Prince' from the Witching Hour flagship store just before Seš'qa Open Day ends! But that's just an evil plot! You caught me right after I stepped into Seš'qa! None of my plot ever happened! I PROMISE! I SWEAR!"
"Here's the guy. A real squealer this one."
I crumpled to the floor with a resounding thud. The room was dark. I carefully opened my eyes and found myself upon a soft and well-fashioned sofa instead of the cold, hard floors of a dungeon.
"Welcome back, my brave adventurer."
A Sarkaz recited the classic line of Errant's Grand Adventure as she took the seat behind a simple stone table. She was decked out in a fine business suit, leather shoes, and a parade mask.

"Mr. Maxim from Asphalt Roundabout. Sixteen years have passed since you last visited Seš'qa. Nevertheless, we have held on to your prize."
Wait. I thought the host of the "Errant's Grand Adventure" was supposed to roleplay as a mysterious diviner witch wearing an ancient courtly dress. Before I could sort out my confusion, a load of bad memories that I had always avoided suddenly surged within me.
I completed the "Errant's Grand Adventure" and could finally exchange my winnings for the final "Treasure from the Lord of Fiends" — a most intricately crafted toy sword. It was my childhood obsession. But I missed the final window for prize exchange when the bells tolled and signaled the end of the Open Day. My old man tried to comfort me and said we'd visit again when the flying city of Seš'qa returns.
But our days at La Fantoma didn't last till the return of Seš'qa. My old man told me that we were broke and gotta head to the Asphalt Roundabout to "dodge the collectors". Of course, I never became a fearless adventurer. Instead, I turned into a petty thief, a dirty smuggler, and a scummy fraud... I only revisited this place because I thought there was a killing to be made.
The Sarkaz then removed the cloth to reveal the prize under it. It sure wasn't a toy sword that blinked and flickered when you pressed a button. It was an actual "Sundered Prince"! An authentic blade painstakingly fashioned by the witch-crafters! Not only that, the Sarkaz manifested a pen out of thin air and signed the weapon for me.
"Are ... are you sure? That prize I missed sixteen years ago... It can't be a weapon of such immense value!"
"I am quite certain. A weapon of a true adventurer, just as revealed by the divination."
"A divination...? Could I ... beg you to divine another reading for me?"
"I'm listening."
"Will my life ... ever get better?"
The Sarkaz looked at me and said something, but the only sound that filled my ears were the tolling of bells that signaled the end of the Open Day that I had not heard for sixteen long years. When I returned to my senses, my legs once again found solid ground. People crowded around me. Everyone, children and grown-ups of every race, looked into the air and waved their goodbyes at the Seš'qa amidst the erupting fireworks. I felt like a young boy again as I gripped my blade. I hoped that the bells tolled a little later. I wanted a sword that flickered and shone when I pressed a button.
But no. I received something that I wanted and I knew exactly what to do.
My grand adventure was about to begin.

8429952182015735354:
"And thus I curse you all. Your blood shall flow across the barren lands and stagnate in pools of gore. Your people and everyone you love shall drown in the filth of your veins."
These were written on the old memo. He thought about it for a while, took out his pen and paper, and started his own scribbles —
"Hardly anyone knows why Landbreakers would want to attack their own people. They used to be a rather cohesive organization led by a single leader... Though the LBs later splintered, such levels of hostility between the successor groups seem to lack a proper basis... The seething hatred so vividly reflected by these notes as well as the brutality personally inflicted upon LBs captured by the author of the said notes can only be described as ... horrifying and gruesome..."
He paused, reflected upon his words, and felt rather displeased with his conclusions. His pen then started scrawling across the page once more.
"I attempted to identify a 'common cause' or 'consensus' in these hateful words. Perhaps the LBs committed a transgression that led to the current kill on sight mentality, or perhaps the LBs have started working on some malicious scheme that continues to this day... I have enjoyed my investigation of the secret truth behind LB history, both as my profession and as a personal pastime of mine. Unfortunately, the author of these notes was just a simple, homicidal brute. He hanged the entire family of his 'breaker-pal' over some petty division of loot."
He paused again briefly, and then continued his scrawls with greater focus and intensity.
"Personal hatred means little. These words provide nothing to my goal of advancing the Security Act for Peace on Talos-II. We must make the very concept of Landbreaking criminal in every law and an anathema to our definitions for civilization. Their presence must be removed from every community, organization, and faction, legal or otherwise. Landbreaking must end to make humanity safe again."
These words seem to lend him strength. His writings picked up speed and alacrity.
"Peripheral rot is a condition that plagues every civilization. This cancer is always there and can never be truly cleansed. However, we must demonstrate our firm resolve and initiate the strong measures to completely sterilize this malignant presence."
He then concluded his own writings with one final line.
"I spent thirty years of my life as a Landbreaker. You must have faith in my words."

8441984886327444814:
The Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) report will display the following statistics: 

<@intru.bold>- Production Efficiency -</>
· Product Info: This displays the types of products that can be produced by the AIC Factory and the size of the local Depot. The Endministrator can star-mark specified products to pin them at the top of the report screen.
· Yield / Usage Curve: Displays the actual yield and usage of a product (in units/min) within a specific range of time.
· Current Data: Actual yield and usage (in units/min) compiled at the current moment.
· Theoretical Data: Theoretical yield and usage (in units/min) calculated based on the number of AIC Factory facilities connected to the grid.

<@intru.bold>- Electrical Efficiency -</>
· Power Grid Report: Total regional PAC power output within a specific time range. Also displays changes in total power consumption of AIC Factory facilities in the region.

※ The Endministrator may sort and filter data in the PAC Report to view the statistics of various regions in various conditions.

Ease of use and expandability are overrated... I tried talking to the operators who gave positive reviews for Jiminy 12. Turned out what they really liked was its ability to focus and amplify the brightness of its Illumination Arts. They said this made field operations a lot easier as the unit could also function as a powerful flashlight. At first, I found these Jiminy 12 reviews laughable and even tried to find a way to convince them that illumination was never part of my design goals and Jiminy 12's resemblance to a flashlight was a mere coincidence. 8528760759423300281: According to mainstream belief, the Aurora of Talos-II comprises 2 parts. The first being the lower portion of the Aurora that exhibits structural and energy profiles similar to those of the Terran Starpod, essentially making this lower portion the Auroral Barrier (though this assumption remains controversial). Fortunately, the Auroral Barrier only surrounds the north polar region and several isolated pockets of Talos-II and does not envelope the entire world like it did on Terra. The second part is the Æther Tide that stretches from the ceiling and origin of the Auroral Barrier to its outer envelope. This Tide would appear as the static Aurora visible to the unaided eye. Æther bursts and flares occur on a regular basis within the Aurora as it spreads from the north pole of Talos-II, leaving powerful Active Blight phenomena in wake.

......
After the Blight eruption disaster in Valley IV, most supplies were delivered by the relevant staff to the doorsteps of the workers' registered addresses. However, many of the locals are still actively foraging for food from the outdoors. Reliable sources revealed that a UWST crew mate named Roman started making flame-roasted Originium slugs at the Valley Pass and offered them, free of charge, to a local temporary shelter that ran low on food. Having heard of this, our correspondent went straight to the site and tried one of the very rustic-looking slug kebab. It turned out to be quite palatable with a soft juicy texture that melts in one's mouth.
......
The Valley IV crew mates are working closely together and helping each other out. There is no question that the Valley will endure these trying times, just as it did twenty years ago.

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher attack that deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> and recovers some SP.

Happy birthday, Endministrator.

I know I've been around you every day but I still decided to send you this mail. Please don't be too surprised. Thanks to your hard work, Endfield Industries has been doing well and staying on course. Everyone is very grateful, and I'm very glad that I can personally celebrate your birthday. I also hope that I will get to personally celebrate this occasion for the upcoming year and in every year that follows.

Your present this year is a cake. I made every effort to keep the sweetness under control. Please do take a bite.

Hmph. That mad woman! I actually grew up with her and gave her many good beat ups. We don't like each other but I like to give her buggly bruises and she tried to give some back. Mad Mimi was seriously mad back then, but okay. I ain't scared of her. Anyway, she later ran off to Wuling, got herself a badge, and even got promo-ed to be a "Watchguard Captain". She thought it gave her the right to put on a stupid stinkface. Yeah, whatever. She can do that stinkface glare all day. I know what's under her stupid watchguard skin. Mad woman. That's who! 
If I fight her again, we probably end up in a draw. But you'll see. Chief Supreme's gonna win eventually. So what if she's got bigger arms? Don't you forget! I've always been faster and wiser and dodgier. I'll give her absolute wreckage. 
Yeah, and about our Wuling raids, that Mad woman's big trouble. The Fam got everything planned out but we just kept running into her at the depot and warehouse and hidey spots. Like she's already waiting for us. We gave a good fight but Mimi's got a nasty crew and threw many of us in da slammer. Worse, she also tried to undermine our Stockade biz by treating the captured Fam really well. Some of the Fam decided to stay in Wuling 'coz the food and living are better. 
You'll see! I'm gonna pawn that mad woman real good. On my own. Alright? Just you wait, mad woman! Chief Supreme is coming to town and putting you at the baby seat! 
— Tangtang, Supreme Chief of Qingbo Stockade 

Yeh, Fam. Mi Fu threw me in jail. I was hauling phat lewt with the Fam when she suddenly appeared in our way. My legs turned to noodles at once. Then she knocked me around a bit but it wasn't that bad. Anyway, she asked: "Wanna stay in town for a while?" Look, man, I was seeing double and there were stars all around me. I didn't know if I nodded or not, but she really dragged me to town. 
At first I be like, man, I was going straight to hell! But later I got a job at town, roof and bed, plus nice food. Then I learned that Mad Mimi be giving us options. She got that mean face and talked like I owed her money, but Mimi probably had to go plow through tons of burdo-shite trying to get some of the Fam decent gigs. 
Oh yeah, you heard, the loot stashes at the grove that we've been nicking from the Wulingers are actually extra supplies from town. I was a bit confused why bother carry the old and obsolete tools to that place. Turned out they were meant for the Stockade. People say Mad Mimi is super tough on the Stockade Fam, but actually she's always helping and caring in her sneaky way. 
— Anonymous, current citizen of Wuling (former Qingbo Stockade resident)

<@profile.key>CODENAME: Endministrator 
GENDER: ■■ 
AUTHENTICATION: ■■ 
DOB: ■■ 
RACE: ■■ 

You remember nothing. 
Your memories lie in fragments, like dreams crushed to dust, disappearing at the slightest thought. 
Sometimes it makes you unsettled, sometimes uneasy, yet it grants you a quiet moment to breathe and think. 
Most of the time, however, there is no pause for doubt. You simply just trust those before you and press on. 
Perhaps you have tucked your past memories away, even if just for a little while... 
...and so, you remember nothing. </>

8695094537645806906:
<@profile.key>Grandma: 
I have received the package from you. The new Arts Unit is just what I need to support my modified orbiter. I have distributed the other local specialties that came attached with the package. There is no need for you to send these things to me. I have a fixed salary good enough that I have some leftover after purchasing parts for engineering mods every month. 
I know you've sent plushes of movie characters, outfits of classic movie stars, and other limited edition movie posters so that I can use these to befriend other individuals. You have been doing this since I was attending the academy — However, those classmates of mine simply stashed the collectibles they received inside their closets and never showed them again. Your following letter told me not to be sad. I really wasn't, grandmother. It is just like what I've told you. Their reactions and responses will not influence my attitude towards them. 
My colleagues at Endfield are not like the classmates I have in the academy. The Endfielders never turned me down nor rejected my ideas. Life is here is good, and I enjoy more liberties. There is something else that I intend to tell you. My communication skills have vastly improved thanks to the instructions provided by my Z7 teammates and constant side-by-side training offered by the Endministrator. 
You once worried that my personality might start to conflict with my superior, but my current situation proves your worries are unnecessary. Our meetings have been enjoyable and the Endmin happily accepts everything I share. This also explains why I have been writing less to you. You no longer need to be concerned about my lack of friends. It is not about taking and giving, but mutual sincerity to explore and discuss everything about each other... I believe I have completely understood the true meaning of "friendship". 
Also, your request for passing on a message to the Endministrator has been carried out by me. The Endmin is enthusiastic and even said that the meeting with you should have taken place much earlier. 

Antal </>
