<@profile.key>Visiting Operator Xaihi during non-working hours is a process so convoluted that people have regarded it as a form of "filter". However, Xaihi herself does not see it that way. The process begins at one's arrival at Dijiang via the TP Point next to the Central Ring. You, the visitor, shall then walk through the lengthy corridors of the living quarters while avoiding the crowd. Make the turn onto the narrow lane, then stop at the unassuming cabin on the right at the very end. The smooth door is almost at the same level with the walls, and seems unwilling to accept entry. You might hesitate and think about rumors of the non-existent "Ghost of Dijiang" as you follow the illustrated instructions and depress the third wall panel next to the door. This is the only action that will reveal the dial hidden beneath. 
Your wait is not long, because the actions of visitation have been permitted by the lady of the alcove. She may exhibit a touch of shyness when she opens the door for you. 
"Revelations of the Law have predicted your arrival," she said as she folded her hands before her skirt and bowed slightly, "I shall seek the object of your desire." 
The door slowly closes before you, denying you visuals of every action and potentiality taking place within that little alcove — the very same location that many operators have esoterically described as "One of the Egresses of the Assimilated Universe". The noise and clamor of Dijiang is only a dozen yards away. You suddenly notice the peculiar tranquility within the narrow corridor. Across immeasurable stretches of time, you, the visitor, is prevented from committing to the flow of verbal exchange. Instead, the collision of stars echo within your ears as teachings and gospels of eons flood your mind. All point toward the nebulous path that yearns to make itself known. 
The door then opens. The gentle Sarkaz girl named Séraphine Xaihi shows herself again. She gives unto you a specialized operational asset that you have applied via the Endfield Office Administration System: A brand new handheld terminal. 
You, the visitor, offer your thanks as you take a quick glance at the alcove interiors. You see a seat showered in the eerie blue of a dozen monitors. There is also a narrow bed for one, blankets neatly folded and moored at its end. And this is all that is contained within "One of the Egresses of the Assimilated Universe". </>

8815245351770140707:
<@profile.key>Operator Ember served in an assault banner of the Order of Steel Oath. She saw active service on the northern front against the Aggeloi before returning to the Civilization Band to manage the posthumous affairs of her late comrades and recruit new soldiers. Following a recommendation from Order Castellan Gerald "The Sanctified" Zasso, Ember began collaborating with Endfield Industries and carrying out various operations on their behalf. 

Ember is a team player and never hesitates in sharing her thoughts. She is also an extremely sensitive listener. As an Oathkeeper soldier, she appreciates the importance of discourse. The duration of this particular interview is about 37% shorter than the average. We recommend adopting the relevant recordings in the Select Examples of Effective Communication of this division. 
Also, pursuant to Supervisor Perlica's requests, the following reminder shall be re-emphasized: Although Ember herself expressed no particular concerns, unnecessary questioning of her past remains an offense to her personal dignity. Anything could have happened in a battlefield, and anyone who endured and survived such extreme adversities shall deserve the utmost respect. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

8881674090098750853:
I refused to agree with Henry's ideas.
The RAYTHEAN motto of making practical products is mere escapism. The form of a weapon is a conclusive result of both traditions and experience. Nature has granted us intuitive senses to differentiate between things that work and things that don't. Elegance and harmony are the unspoken answers for perfection that we arrived at thanks to the countless years of our history and hard-earned experiences.
And I must seize this bid just to prove this point.

I got 9 ways to achieve the level of destruction desired by the client, but none of them should be used by a weapon of this size.
I also heard that Henry decided to test out his flash-burn impulse technology. Laughable. The client specifically requested a sword, not a cannon with a hilt welded to it.

Weapon performance failed to meet the desired specifications.
Henry had a short discussion with me. His team was also out of ideas. I didn't know what the client might think if they saw this private meeting of weapon designers from 2 competing companies. 

Their specifications were impossible. Why did the boss agree to take on this contract?
Even if the dude took the sample prototype to HQ and wanted something with the exact same performance, he would just get another strange artifact unearthed by another bunch of weirdos. We now have more than reputation to worry about. These untested and strange devices pose a threat to our very lives.

I received a flash of inspiration during shower that might just help us meet the minimum specs. I knew bathroom opera would work.
A stack of 4 core armor slabs of armored vehicles? Hmph. My solution could go through that in a single slash. I doubt if RAYTHEAN has a better alternative. Physics is a hard science with hard limits. We may have our differences, but both of us should acknowledge this truth.

The Cabal of Tranquility re-activated the prototype.
It went through ... a stack of 16 armor slabs. The Cabal intends to run another test with a stack of 32 once emergency repairs for the ceiling is complete.
They even proposed a diagonal cut from corner to corner.
Maybe I should talk to the boss about this.

8945053560384376439:
<@profile.key>Ekut is the actual name of Operator Arclight. Whenever people ask if she came from the Hannabit Circuit, Arclight always answered with this line: "I follow Aldertone Orisha, and every Songseeker is her own master." 
Operator Arclight has an indescribable passion about motorcycles. Arsenal Engineer Semyon often conversed with Arclight regarding her motorcycle, and these are what he had to say: 
"Initially, I thought it was a custom military vehicle that found its way into the civilian market. I could hardly believe what I saw when it turned out to be a work of engineering that Arclight assembled and tuned on her own. I studied the motorcycle closely after acquiring her permission. Its Originium engine was a ten-year-old piece of obsolete tech from Mieszko Industries. The drivetrain was produced by Triglava Armoury. Labels on the suspensions were worn out, but I guessed, based on the structures, that they were from Northmarch Support Hub. The most impressive component would be the chassis — the entire thing was handcrafted, and every weld made to the greatest detail. The entire framework felt almost seamless. Even more extraordinary was the fact that the motorcycle wasn't just an assembly, but an integration that exemplified the strengths of every component's manufacturer. This could only have been achieved with the highest level of technical expertise. The entire vehicle was a near-perfect balance of power, endurance, and handling." 
Although everyone approves of Operator Arclight's superb technical abilities, many also pointed out this observation: She treated people rather coldly, and spoke in a manner that was rather difficult to understand. Even Operator Semyon reflected that Arclight rarely spoke. Perhaps Operator Arclight's unique experiences made her appear lukewarm, and her first impressions often gave off the vibe of an unfriendly individual. However, people who got to know her better would learn that she was an incredibly reasonable person. Those who often converse with Arclight even heard her saying shocking things that resembled jokes. Needless to say, Operator Arclight was simply speaking her mind and wasn't trying to be funny in these instances. 
Comments about Arclight being difficult to converse with typically arise as her language was heavily influenced by the Hannabit Circuit's unique form of expressions. Arclight has a tendency to use metaphors inspired by the natural world, such as using burdenbeast temper to describe mechanical breakdowns, or the morning mist to illustrate an individual's hesitation. People who are more forthright in their manners of speech may be thrown off by Arclight's unique linguistic choices. However, good listeners would appreciate her clear reasoning and steadfast consistency in her actions. Songseekers of the Hannabit Circuit have their own unique understanding of the world and modus operandi. If one wishes to understand Arclight better, one would do well to appreciate her way of describing this world. </>

<@profile.key>Operator Yvonne was recommended by Crowe of UWST to Andre. She joined the Specialist Tech Division of Endfield Industries via the "Specialized Talent Recruitment" channel. Yvonne is now leading several research projects on the Æther, the Blight, and the Trans-Æther Tranquilizer-Automata associated with the previous two. Given her outstanding scientific research capabilities, she has received bonus grants and resources to support her independent research. 

During background checks, many have described Yvonne as an engineer with an obvious streak of "rebellion". However, I cannot be more opposed to this evaluation. And no, this has nothing to do with the laser stickers she adhered upon my chassis. 
In my opinion, the core issue rests in the inability of most individuals to keep up with her rapid train of thought. The end result would appear that she has teamwork issues. Also, she is rather confident in her personal style that she constructed for herself after years of scientific research... Her violations were never deliberate. Instead, they should be considered instinctive reactions when someone or something attempts to interfere with her way of thinking or self-expression. 
Such personality quirks withstood the influences of the environment and many would often regard this as Yvonne's rebel spirit. However, her brightly colored outfits, carefree graffiti, and complex yet extremely practical chassis designs are modes of expression that she has consistently maintained — in a world filled with rules and boundaries, those who remain true to themselves would seem rather out of place. Fortunately, Endfield is not a place dictated by inflexible regulations. Our vision is to change this world, instead of sustaining its current existence by sticking to the law. 
Moreover, when our people described Yvonne as a "rebel", they did so with a smile. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

9180182437882080626:
<@profile.key>Tangtang is a member and primary representative of Qingbo Stockade. She joined Endfield Industries for personal reasons and is currently serving as Head of Endfield's Qingbo Stockade Liaison Office.

Operator Tangtang stands as perhaps the most distinctive individual among the diverse personnel the Endministrator has invited aboard Dijiang. Much like our first meeting... Apologies, I need to retrieve the recorded footage stored in my database:
"Thwack! Thwack!"
"Hey, what's this thing doing on a big airship? Looks like a three-headed iron tuskbeast. Kinda dumb if you ask me..."
"Operator Tangtang, I'm here for your onboarding inter— No, please don't slap my panel. I'm a personnel assistant from Endfield Industries..."
"So you're one of Darkmask's crew! You should've said so sooner, Old Iron-face! Here, I've got some good stuff I brought for my new Fam. Go crazy!"
After I repeatedly insisted I had no need for any Tangtang Certs, ores, or jewelry, Tangtang enthusiastically pulled a ring-shaped ornament made from natural materials from her pocket. I believe it could be defined as a woven bamboo floral wreath. She placed the wreath over one of my panels, then curiously began tapping the other two: "You're sharper than those Wuling tin toys! Why don't you come join me in Qingbo Stockade? Come on, call me Chief!"
My assessment of Tangtang aligns with others': cheerful, straightforward, unpretentious. She possesses a childlike heart, brimming with passion and curiosity toward everything... If I had to summarize her in a few words, I'd choose "a child in the midst of growing up." However, I absolutely cannot join Qingbo Stockade and conquer Talos-II with her... This has nothing to do with the endless stack of personnel files I have to process. It just doesn't quite align with Endfield's regulations.
To avoid injuring Tangtang's hands and to prevent myself from suffering "mechanical" concussion, I really should consider how to stay out of her way... Strangely enough, she always manages to find me.
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>

9204288396684466538:
The Sankta navigated through the battlefield and stopped by a severely injured UWST crew mate. She stooped over and made a cursory check of the wounds. The crew mate was unlikely to survive until the arrival of a qualified medic.

"May you finally find eternal rest. Aeternam habeas requiem."

The Sankta held his hands and prayed that the poor soul may find peace.
How many like him had she found? Thirty? Or three hundred? The Sankta tried to remember as she held the hands that were already growing cold. She felt the rocks and dirt move behind her and drew her handcannon at once — An Aggelos, with a flickering halo above its head.
The Sankta slowly approached the paralyzed Aggelos. It was no longer a threat. Half of its body was already pulverized by the artillery of the Worker's Guard.

She had a question that she knew could not be explained by the things she saw in this place. Ever since the Cabal of Tranquility activated its Nomadic Monastery upon Talos-II, and ever since the Sankta witnessed the first Aggelos, they — we were destined to seek the answers on our own, for none could ever respond to that question.

"By the graces of the Law, lead me to the journey home..."

Every Sankta of the Cabal knew how to sing this anthem. They sang the song as they bathed in the gazes of the saints and crossed the Cosmic Gate. They sang the same song when they traversed the ruins scattered across the world of Talos-II.
She still remembered the story that Abbess Vronta said during morning prayer: During ancient times that predate the oldest annals, the Teekaz who sought the Lord's protection became the Sankta. And during the time when the Lord left the Temple, the First Saint revealed her Teekaz form to the Sankta. But this story only filled her with confusion.
If the halo was the Lord's exclusive gift to the Sankta and only the Sankta, then what about the hosts of lifeless yet animate constructs called the Aggeloi of Talos-II? Where did their halos come from?

Or rather, who was the one that made you?

Sankta gripped her gun tightly. No one could answer her question. The only thing that felt real was the weapon in her hand.
Soon, the final Ankhor disappeared from the horizon. The steam whistle of victory echoed throughout the battle lines. Once again the UWST pioneers pushed deeper into the frontier. This was the moment when the Civilization Band finds another secure footing in the desolate lands.
While those around her cheered, the Sankta placed both her hands on the head of the "dying" Aggelos. She thought she had felt something pulse within the supposedly unfeeling abomination ... but the feeling quickly gave way to empty silence.
The Lord may have blessed the Sankta with their halos, but this blessing may have been granted to others. She kept thinking about what this might mean until faith once again filled her heart. It was at this moment, and at this very place, that the Sankta from the Cabal must perform another mass for the departed.

"No matter where you are from, may you finally find eternal rest. Aeternam habeas requiem."

The rocks cracked and disintegrated as dust leaked out through the gaps between the Sankta's fingers. The flickering halo finally dissipated into the air.

9218863353266104045:
<@profile.key>[Old Video Footage]
06:23:45:10
"A camera ... the gimmick that records stuff? Looks pretty cute, and it can even move. Looks like a little Lupo!"
"The Wulingers came up with this gadget. We snagged it during our last run-in with 'em. Just gotta hit this button here to fire it up... Hey, Chief, wanna take a shot and see how it looks?"
"Come on, let's do it! Hey, how'd you manage to make my legs look so short in the shot? Makes your boss look tiny!"
"Ain't you just naturally kinda— Hey hey, no hitting! It's me, I'm the short one, I'm short okay!"
"Not playing with you anymore! I'm gonna wallop that mad woman and use this 'lil' Lupo to record her getting knocked flat!"

07:10:30:15
"Damn that mad woman, off fighting for her big madam Zhuang again... Last time she got beaten black and blue by splatters, you'd think she'd learn...
"I'll just cozy up in my stockade and dodge the blightery out there... Got clean water to drink and delicious fin to eat...
"Hey, lil' Lupo, hold still! Look! Some clueless girl's practicing swordplay near the Wuling border. Let's tail her and see what's up.
"Heh, those twin swords of hers look pretty decent. The local turf has too many splatters and getting pretty dangerous. Better not let her end up as splatter-chow. And I the Chief shall take her fancy stuff off her hands."

08:36:05:55
"You took out all these splatters yourself?"
"Yep, these little buggers were no match for me. But hey, thanks for the backup! Your gunplay's pretty solid. I'm Chen Qianyu, and nice meeting you...?"
"Tangtang. Supreme Chief of Qingbo Stockade! Ever heard of me?"
"Of course! Qingbo Stockade, the ones always stirring up trouble with Wuling City... Hold up, you're not here to rob me, are you?"
"That depends. Are you a Wulinger? Your Chief is just and Wuling is the only thing on my hate list!"
"I, uh, kinda am ... wait, no. Not now. Not anymore..."
"WHAT? Did mad woman kick you out? It's fine, being a Wulinger sucks blight — Hey, why are you crying? ARGH! Don't wipe your snot on me!"

11:42:13:07
"Didn't score any treasure, and even lost a piece of clothing. This trip's a total bust... WOAH?!"
"Hey, little kitty, loitering around Wuling again? Thought I told you to skip this place."
"None of your business, mad woman! Let me go right now!"

Tangtang and Chen Qianyu huddled close as they examined the camera footage. They looked each other, bewildered and quiet. Suddenly, Tangtang snatched the device and bolted off at full speed—
"Darkmask! Pale Scribe! TAKE A LOOK AT THIS! I got some awesome vids of Green Scales' crying face! AND THEY'RE BUGGLY!"
"Tangtang!!! Stop right there!!!"
Chen Qianyu, face flushed with embarrassment, chased Tangtang around the Central Ring five or six times until Mi Fu, unable to watch any longer, grabbed and lifted both of them by the collar.
"Tangtang, I had to chase you down in Wuling, and I still have to chase you down aboard Dijiang during an official trip... Chen, why are you enabling her mischief?"
"My fellow Fam Darkmask owns this place, mad woman! You got no authority here! Let your Chief go! NOW!"
While Tangtang and Mi Fu traded verbal blows, Chen Qianyu smoothly slipped free from Mi Fu's grip, snatched the camera from Tangtang's hands, and promptly pressed the delete button on that piece of "shame." Then, sticking her tongue out playfully, she turned the lens toward the bickering duo.
The camera faithfully captured the scene. Tangtang, held by the scruff of her neck by Mi Fu, hearkened back to the past — Despite the countless chases and scuffles, Mi Fu never grabbed Tangtang by her vitals. As long as Tangtang got her act together, she could always find a chance to escape.
Tangtang never figured out why she always slipped away so easily. The observant Chen Qianyu who saw this naturally became the prize-winner of this little tussle: a video of Tangtang the Defeated Chief with drooping ears and utterly pitiful looks, one that can be enjoyed over and over again.</>
